{{Infobox religious building | name = Kitaya Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-01.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}}, {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18年}} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in {{ill|September|ja|9月}}) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3年}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|ja|1810年}} and {{ill|1830|ja|1830年}}){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Bunmei era (1469–1486)|ja|文明}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|ja|1997年}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kikutake Kiyonori|ja|菊竹清訓}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}}) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}) * {{ill|Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命}} (a deity of {{ill|longevity|ja|長寿}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|ja|NHK放送センター}} and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42年}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|ja|1920年}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} * {{ill|Udagawachō|ja|宇田川町}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅}} (major terminal station) : Served by: * {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}: {{ill|Yamanote Line|ja|山手線}}, {{ill|Saikyō Line|ja|埼京線}}, {{ill|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line|ja|湘南新宿ライン}}, {{ill|Narita Express|ja|成田エクスプレス}} * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄}}: {{ill|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|ja|東急東横線}}, {{ill|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|ja|東急田園都市線}} * {{ill|Keiō Corporation|ja|京王電鉄}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ}}: {{ill|Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}}, {{ill|Hanzōmon Line|ja|東京メトロ半蔵門線}}, {{ill|Fukutoshin Line|ja|東京メトロ副都心線}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷}} - {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} - {{ill|Shibuya Ward Office|ja|渋谷区役所}} * {{ill|Shinto|ja|神社}} - {{ill|Tokyo Shrine Association|ja|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|Deities in Shinto|ja|deity}} - {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} - {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} * {{ill|Spain-zaka|ja|スペイン坂}} * {{ill|Cultural Village Street|ja|文化村通り}} * {{ill|Dogenzaka|ja|道玄坂}} * {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/shibuya/2065/|Kitaya Inari Shrine}} (Tokyo Shrine Association) {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}}, {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18年}} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in {{ill|September|ja|9月}}) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3年}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|ja|1810年}} and {{ill|1830|ja|1830年}}){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Bunmei era (1469–1486)|ja|文明}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|ja|1997年}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kikutake Kiyonori|ja|菊竹清訓}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}}) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}) * {{ill|Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命}} (a deity of {{ill|longevity|ja|長寿}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|ja|NHK放送センター}} and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42年}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|ja|1920年}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} * {{ill|Udagawachō|ja|宇田川町}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅}} (major terminal station) : Served by: * {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}: {{ill|Yamanote Line|ja|山手線}}, {{ill|Saikyō Line|ja|埼京線}}, {{ill|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line|ja|湘南新宿ライン}}, {{ill|Narita Express|ja|成田エクスプレス}} * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄}}: {{ill|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|ja|東急東横線}}, {{ill|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|ja|東急田園都市線}} * {{ill|Keiō Corporation|ja|京王電鉄}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ}}: {{ill|Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}}, {{ill|Hanzōmon Line|ja|東京メトロ半蔵門線}}, {{ill|Fukutoshin Line|ja|東京メトロ副都心線}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{Infobox religious building | name = Kitaya Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-01.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}}, {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18年}} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in {{ill|September|ja|9月}}) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3年}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|ja|1810年}} and {{ill|1830|ja|1830年}}){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Bunmei era (1469–1486)|ja|文明}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|ja|1997年}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kikutake Kiyonori|ja|菊竹清訓}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}}) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}) * {{ill|Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命}} (a deity of {{ill|longevity|ja|長寿}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|ja|NHK放送センター}} and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42年}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|ja|1920年}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} * {{ill|Udagawachō|ja|宇田川町}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅}} (major terminal station) : Served by: * {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}: {{ill|Yamanote Line|ja|山手線}}, {{ill|Saikyō Line|ja|埼京線}}, {{ill|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line|ja|湘南新宿ライン}}, {{ill|Narita Express|ja|成田エクスプレス}} * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄}}: {{ill|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|ja|東急東横線}}, {{ill|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|ja|東急田園都市線}} * {{ill|Keiō Corporation|ja|京王電鉄}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ}}: {{ill|Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}}, {{ill|Hanzōmon Line|ja|東京メトロ半蔵門線}}, {{ill|Fukutoshin Line|ja|東京メトロ副都心線}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷}} - {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} - {{ill|Shibuya Ward Office|ja|渋谷区役所}} * {{ill|Shinto|ja|神社}} - {{ill|Tokyo Shrine Association|ja|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|Deities in Shinto|ja|deity}} - {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} - {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} * {{ill|Spain-zaka|ja|スペイン坂}} * {{ill|Cultural Village Street|ja|文化村通り}} * {{ill|Dogenzaka|ja|道玄坂}} * {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/shibuya/2065/|Kitaya Inari Shrine}} (Tokyo Shrine Association) 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅}} (major terminal station) : Served by: * {{ill|JR revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Kitaya Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-01.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}}, {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18年}} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in {{ill|September|ja|9月}}) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3年}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|ja|1810年}} and {{ill|1830|ja|1830年}}){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Bunmei era (1469–1486)|ja|文明}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|ja|1997年}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kikutake Kiyonori|ja|菊竹清訓}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}}) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}) * {{ill|Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命}} (a deity of {{ill|longevity|ja|長寿}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|ja|NHK放送センター}} and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42年}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|ja|1920年}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} * {{ill|Udagawachō|ja|宇田川町}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅}} (major terminal station) : Served by: * {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}: {{ill|Yamanote Line|ja|山手線}}, {{ill|Saikyō Line|ja|埼京線}}, {{ill|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line|ja|湘南新宿ライン}}, {{ill|Narita Express|ja|成田エクスプレス}} * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄}}: {{ill|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|ja|東急東横線}}, {{ill|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|ja|東急田園都市線}} * {{ill|Keiō Corporation|ja|京王電鉄}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ}}: {{ill|Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}}, {{ill|Hanzōmon Line|ja|東京メトロ半蔵門線}}, {{ill|Fukutoshin Line|ja|東京メトロ副都心線}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷}} - {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} - {{ill|Shibuya Ward Office|ja|渋谷区役所}} * {{ill|Shinto|ja|神社}} - {{ill|Tokyo Shrine Association|ja|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|Deities in Shinto|ja|deity}} - {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} - {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} * {{ill|Spain-zaka|ja|スペイン坂}} * {{ill|Cultural Village Street|ja|文化村通り}} * {{ill|Dogenzaka|ja|道玄坂}} * {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/shibuya/2065/|Kitaya Inari Shrine}} (Tokyo Shrine Association) {{Infobox religious building | name = Kitaya Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-01.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}}, {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18年}} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in {{ill|September|ja|9月}}) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3年}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|ja|1810年}} and {{ill|1830|ja|1830年}}){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Bunmei era (1469–1486)|ja|文明}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|ja|1997年}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kikutake Kiyonori|ja|菊竹清訓}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}}) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}) * {{ill|Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命}} (a deity of {{ill|longevity|ja|長寿}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|ja|NHK放送センター}} and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42年}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|ja|1920年}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} * {{ill|Udagawachō|ja|宇田川町}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅}} (major terminal station) : Served by: * {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}: {{ill|Yamanote Line|ja|山手線}}, {{ill|Saikyō Line|ja|埼京線}}, {{ill|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line|ja|湘南新宿ライン}}, {{ill|Narita Express|ja|成田エクスプレス}} * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄}}: {{ill|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|ja|東急東横線}}, {{ill|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|ja|東急田園都市線}} * {{ill|Keiō Corporation|ja|京王電鉄}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ}}: {{ill|Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}}, {{ill|Hanzōmon Line|ja|東京メトロ半蔵門線}}, {{ill|Fukutoshin Line|ja|東京メトロ副都心線}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷}} - {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} - {{ill|Shibuya Ward Office|ja|渋谷区役所}} * {{ill|Shinto|ja|神社}} - {{ill|Tokyo Shrine Association|ja|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|Deities in Shinto|ja|deity}} - {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} - {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} * {{ill|Spain-zaka|ja|スペイン坂}} * {{ill|Cultural Village Street|ja|文化村通り}} * {{ill|Dogenzaka|ja|道玄坂}} * {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/shibuya/2065/|Kitaya Inari Shrine}} (Tokyo Shrine Association) {{Infobox religious building | name = Kitaya Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-01.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}}, {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18年}} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in {{ill|September|ja|9月}}) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3年}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|ja|1810年}} and {{ill|1830|ja|1830年}}){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Bunmei era (1469–1486)|ja|文明}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|ja|1997年}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kikutake Kiyonori|ja|菊竹清訓}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}}) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}) * {{ill|Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命}} (a deity of {{ill|longevity|ja|長寿}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|ja|NHK放送センター}} and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42年}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|ja|1920年}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} * {{ill|Udagawachō|ja|宇田川町}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅}} (major terminal station) : Served by: * {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}: {{ill|Yamanote Line|ja|山手線}}, {{ill|Saikyō Line|ja|埼京線}}, {{ill|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line|ja|湘南新宿ライン}}, {{ill|Narita Express|ja|成田エクスプレス}} * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄}}: {{ill|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|ja|東急東横線}}, {{ill|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|ja|東急田園都市線}} * {{ill|Keiō Corporation|ja|京王電鉄}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ}}: {{ill|Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}}, {{ill|Hanzōmon Line|ja|東京メトロ半蔵門線}}, {{ill|Fukutoshin Line|ja|東京メトロ副都心線}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷}} - {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} - {{ill|Shibuya Ward Office|ja|渋谷区役所}} * {{ill|Shinto|ja|神社}} - {{ill|Tokyo Shrine Association|ja|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|Deities in Shinto|ja|deity}} - {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} - {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} * {{ill|Spain-zaka|ja|スペイン坂}} * {{ill|Cultural Village Street|ja|文化村通り}} * {{ill|Dogenzaka|ja|道玄坂}} * {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/shibuya/2065/|Kitaya Inari Shrine}} (Tokyo Shrine Association) Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}}, {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18年}} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in {{ill|September|ja|9月}}) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3年}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|ja|1810年}} and {{ill|1830|ja|1830年}}){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Bunmei era (1469–1486)|ja|文明}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|ja|1997年}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kikutake Kiyonori|ja|菊竹清訓}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}}) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}) * {{ill|Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命}} (a deity of {{ill|longevity|ja|長寿}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|ja|NHK放送センター}} and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42年}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|ja|1920年}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} * {{ill|Udagawachō|ja|宇田川町}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅}} (major terminal station) : Served by: * {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}: {{ill|Yamanote Line|ja|山手線}}, {{ill|Saikyō Line|ja|埼京線}}, {{ill|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line|ja|湘南新宿ライン}}, {{ill|Narita Express|ja|成田エクスプレス}} * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄}}: {{ill|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|ja|東急東横線}}, {{ill|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|ja|東急田園都市線}} * {{ill|Keiō Corporation|ja|京王電鉄}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ}}: {{ill|Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}}, {{ill|Hanzōmon Line|ja|東京メトロ半蔵門線}}, {{ill|Fukutoshin Line|ja|東京メトロ副都心線}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷}} - {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} - {{ill|Shibuya Ward Office|ja|渋谷区役所}} * {{ill|Shinto|ja|神社}} - {{ill|Tokyo Shrine Association|ja|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|Deities in Shinto|ja|deity}} - {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} - {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} * {{ill|Spain-zaka|ja|スペイン坂}} * {{ill|Cultural Village Street|ja|文化村通り}} * {{ill|Dogenzaka|ja|道玄坂}} * {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/shibuya/2065/|Kitaya Inari Shrine}} (Tokyo Shrine Association) {{Infobox religious building | name = Kitaya Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-01.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}}, {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18年}} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in {{ill|September|ja|9月}}) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3年}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|ja|1810年}} and {{ill|1830|ja|1830年}}){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Bunmei era (1469–1486)|ja|文明}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|ja|1997年}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kikutake Kiyonori|ja|菊竹清訓}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}}) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}) * {{ill|Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命}} (a deity of {{ill|longevity|ja|長寿}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|ja|NHK放送センター}} and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42年}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|ja|1920年}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} * {{ill|Udagawachō|ja|宇田川町}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅}} (major terminal station) : Served by: * {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}: {{ill|Yamanote Line|ja|山手線}}, {{ill|Saikyō Line|ja|埼京線}}, {{ill|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line|ja|湘南新宿ライン}}, {{ill|Narita Express|ja|成田エクスプレス}} * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄}}: {{ill|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|ja|東急東横線}}, {{ill|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|ja|東急田園都市線}} * {{ill|Keiō Corporation|ja|京王電鉄}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ}}: {{ill|Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}}, {{ill|Hanzōmon Line|ja|東京メトロ半蔵門線}}, {{ill|Fukutoshin Line|ja|東京メトロ副都心線}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷}} - {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} - {{ill|Shibuya Ward Office|ja|渋谷区役所}} * {{ill|Shinto|ja|神社}} - {{ill|Tokyo Shrine Association|ja|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|Deities in Shinto|ja|deity}} - {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} - {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} * {{ill|Spain-zaka|ja|スペイン坂}} * {{ill|Cultural Village Street|ja|文化村通り}} * Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}}, {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18年}} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in {{ill|September|ja|9月}}) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3年}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|ja|1810年}} and {{ill|1830|ja|1830年}}){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Bunmei era (1469–1486)|ja|文明}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|ja|1997年}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kikutake Kiyonori|ja|菊竹清訓}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}}) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}) * {{ill|Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命}} (a deity of {{ill|longevity|ja|長寿}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|ja|NHK放送センター}} and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42年}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|ja|1920年}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} * {{ill|Udagawachō|ja|宇田川町}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅}} (major terminal station) : Served by: * {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}: {{ill|Yamanote Line|ja|山手線}}, {{ill|Saikyō Line|ja|埼京線}}, {{ill|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line|ja|湘南新宿ライン}}, {{ill|Narita Express|ja|成田エクスプレス}} * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄}}: {{ill|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|ja|東急東横線}}, {{ill|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|ja|東急田園都市線}} * {{ill|Keiō Corporation|ja|京王電鉄}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ}}: {{ill|Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}}, {{ill|Hanzōmon Line|ja|東京メトロ半蔵門線}}, {{ill|Fukutoshin Line|ja|東京メトロ副都心線}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷}} - {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} - {{ill|Shibuya Ward Office|ja|渋谷区役所}} * {{ill|Shinto|ja|神社}} - {{ill|Tokyo Shrine Association|ja|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|Deities in Shinto|ja|deity}} - {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} - {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} * {{ill|Spain-zaka|ja|スペイン坂}} * {{ill|Cultural Village Street|ja|文化村通り}} * {{ill|Dogenzaka|ja|道玄坂}} * {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/shibuya/2065/|Kitaya Inari Shrine}} (Tokyo Shrine Association) {{Infobox religious building | name = Kitaya Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-01.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}}, {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18年}} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in {{ill|September|ja|9月}}) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3年}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|ja|1810年}} and {{ill|1830|ja|1830年}}){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Bunmei era (1469–1486)|ja|文明}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|ja|1997年}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kikutake Kiyonori|ja|菊竹清訓}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}}) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}) * {{ill|Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命}} (a deity of {{ill|longevity|ja|長寿}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|ja|NHK放送センター}} and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42年}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|ja|1920年}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} * {{ill|Udagawachō|ja|宇田川町}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅}} (major terminal station) : Served by: * {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}: {{ill|Yamanote Line|ja|山手線}}, {{ill|Saikyō Line|ja|埼京線}}, {{ill|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line|ja|湘南新宿ライン}}, {{ill|Narita Express|ja|成田エクスプレス}} * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄}}: {{ill|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|ja|東急東横線}}, {{ill|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|ja|東急田園都市線}} * {{ill|Keiō Corporation|ja|京王電鉄}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ}}: {{ill|Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}}, {{ill|Hanzōmon Line|ja|東京メトロ半蔵門線}}, {{ill|Fukutoshin Line|ja|東京メトロ副都心線}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷}} - {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} - {{ill|Shibuya Ward Office|ja|渋谷区役所}} * {{ill|Shinto|ja|神社}} - {{ill|Tokyo Shrine Association|ja|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|Deities in Shinto|ja|deity}} - {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} - {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} * {{ill|Spain-zaka|ja|スペイン坂}} * {{ill|Cultural Village Street|ja|文化村通り}} * Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}}, {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18年}} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in {{ill|September|ja|9月}}) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3年}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|ja|1810年}} and {{ill|1830|ja|1830年}}){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Bunmei era (1469–1486)|ja|文明}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|ja|1997年}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kikutake Kiyonori|ja|菊竹清訓}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}}) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}) * {{ill|Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命}} (a deity of {{ill|longevity|ja|長寿}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|ja|NHK放送センター}} and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42年}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|ja|1920年}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} * {{ill|Udagawachō|ja|宇田川町}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅}} (major terminal station) : Served by: * {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}: {{ill|Yamanote Line|ja|山手線}}, {{ill|Saikyō Line|ja|埼京線}}, {{ill|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line|ja|湘南新宿ライン}}, {{ill|Narita Express|ja|成田エクスプレス}} * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄}}: {{ill|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|ja|東急東横線}}, {{ill|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|ja|東急田園都市線}} * {{ill|Keiō Corporation|ja|京王電鉄}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ}}: {{ill|Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}}, {{ill|Hanzōmon Line|ja|東京メトロ半蔵門線}}, {{ill|Fukutoshin Line|ja|東京メトロ副都心線}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷}} - {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} - {{ill|Shibuya Ward Office|ja|渋谷区役所}} * {{ill|Shinto|ja|神社}} - {{ill|Tokyo Shrine Association|ja|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|Deities in Shinto|ja|deity}} - {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} - {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} * {{ill|Spain-zaka|ja|スペイン坂}} * {{ill|Cultural Village Street|ja|文化村通り}} * Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}}, {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18年}} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in {{ill|September|ja|9月}}) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3年}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|ja|1810年}} and {{ill|1830|ja|1830年}}){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Bunmei era (1469–1486)|ja|文明}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|ja|1997年}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kikutake Kiyonori|ja|菊竹清訓}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}}) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}) * {{ill|Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命}} (a deity of {{ill|longevity|ja|長寿}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|ja|NHK放送センター}} and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42年}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|ja|1920年}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} * {{ill|Udagawachō|ja|宇田川町}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅}} (major terminal station) : Served by: * {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}: {{ill|Yamanote Line|ja|山手線}}, {{ill|Saikyō Line|ja|埼京線}}, {{ill|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line|ja|湘南新宿ライン}}, {{ill|Narita Express|ja|成田エクスプレス}} * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄}}: {{ill|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|ja|東急東横線}}, {{ill|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|ja|東急田園都市線}} * {{ill|Keiō Corporation|ja|京王電鉄}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ}}: {{ill|Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}}, {{ill|Hanzōmon Line|ja|東京メトロ半蔵門線}}, {{ill|Fukutoshin Line|ja|東京メトロ副都心線}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷}} - {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} - {{ill|Shibuya Ward Office|ja|渋谷区役所}} * {{ill|Shinto|ja|神社}} - {{ill|Tokyo Shrine Association|ja|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|Deities in Shinto|ja|deity}} - {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} - {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} * {{ill|Spain-zaka|ja|スペイン坂}} * {{ill|Cultural Village Street|ja|文化村通り}} * {{ill|Dogenzaka|ja|道玄坂}} * {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/shibuya/2065/|Kitaya Inari Shrine}} (Tokyo Shrine Association) Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}}, {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18年}} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in {{ill|September|ja|9月}}) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3年}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|ja|1810年}} and {{ill|1830|ja|1830年}}){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Bunmei era (1469–1486)|ja|文明}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|ja|1997年}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kikutake Kiyonori|ja|菊竹清訓}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}}) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}) * {{ill|Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命}} (a deity of {{ill|longevity|ja|長寿}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|ja|NHK放送センター}} and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42年}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|ja|1920年}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} * {{ill|Udagawachō|ja|宇田川町}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅}} (major terminal station) : Served by: * {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}: {{ill|Yamanote Line|ja|山手線}}, {{ill|Saikyō Line|ja|埼京線}}, {{ill|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line|ja|湘南新宿ライン}}, {{ill|Narita Express|ja|成田エクスプレス}} * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄}}: {{ill|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|ja|東急東横線}}, {{ill|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|ja|東急田園都市線}} * {{ill|Keiō Corporation|ja|京王電鉄}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ}}: {{ill|Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}}, {{ill|Hanzōmon Line|ja|東京メトロ半蔵門線}}, {{ill|Fukutoshin Line|ja|東京メトロ副都心線}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷}} - {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} - {{ill|Shibuya Ward Office|ja|渋谷区役所}} * {{ill|Shinto|ja|神社}} - {{ill|Tokyo Shrine Association|ja|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|Deities in Shinto|ja|deity}} - {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} - {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} * {{ill|Spain-zaka|ja|スペイン坂}} * {{ill|Cultural Village Street|ja|文化村通り}} * {{ill|Dogenzaka|ja|道玄坂}} * {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/shibuya/2065/|Kitaya Inari Shrine}} (Tokyo Shrine Association) {{Infobox religious building | name = Kitaya Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-01.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}}, {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18年}} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in {{ill|September|ja|9月}}) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3年}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|ja|1810年}} and {{ill|1830|ja|1830年}}){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Bunmei era (1469–1486)|ja|文明}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|ja|1997年}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kikutake Kiyonori|ja|菊竹清訓}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}}) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}) * {{ill|Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命}} (a deity of {{ill|longevity|ja|長寿}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|ja|NHK放送センター}} and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42年}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|ja|1920年}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} * {{ill|Udagawachō|ja|宇田川町}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅}} (major terminal station) : Served by: * {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}: {{ill|Yamanote Line|ja|山手線}}, {{ill|Saikyō Line|ja|埼京線}}, {{ill|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line|ja|湘南新宿ライン}}, {{ill|Narita Express|ja|成田エクスプレス}} * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄}}: {{ill|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|ja|東急東横線}}, {{ill|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|ja|東急田園都市線}} * {{ill|Keiō Corporation|ja|京王電鉄}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ}}: {{ill|Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}}, {{ill|Hanzōmon Line|ja|東京メトロ半蔵門線}}, {{ill|Fukutoshin Line|ja|東京メトロ副都心線}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷}} - {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} - {{ill|Shibuya Ward Office|ja|渋谷区役所}} * {{ill|Shinto|ja|神社}} - {{ill|Tokyo Shrine Association|ja|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|Deities in Shinto|ja|deity}} - {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} - {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} * {{ill|Spain-zaka|ja|スペイン坂}} * {{ill|Cultural Village Street|ja|文化村通り}} * {{ill|Dogenzaka|ja|道玄坂}} * {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/shibuya/2065/|Kitaya Inari Shrine}} (Tokyo Shrine Association) {{Infobox religious building | name = Kitaya Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-01.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}}, {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18年}} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in {{ill|September|ja|9月}}) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3年}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|ja|1810年}} and {{ill|1830|ja|1830年}}){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Bunmei era (1469–1486)|ja|文明}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|ja|1997年}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kikutake Kiyonori|ja|菊竹清訓}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}}) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}) * {{ill|Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命}} (a deity of {{ill|longevity|ja|長寿}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|ja|NHK放送センター}} and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42年}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|ja|1920年}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} * {{ill|Udagawachō|ja|宇田川町}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅}} (major terminal station) : Served by: * {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}: {{ill|Yamanote Line|ja|山手線}}, {{ill|Saikyō Line|ja|埼京線}}, {{ill|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line|ja|湘南新宿ライン}}, {{ill|Narita Express|ja|成田エクスプレス}} * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄}}: {{ill|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|ja|東急東横線}}, {{ill|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|ja|東急田園都市線}} * {{ill|Keiō Corporation|ja|京王電鉄}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ}}: {{ill|Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}}, {{ill|Hanzōmon Line|ja|東京メトロ半蔵門線}}, {{ill|Fukutoshin Line|ja|東京メトロ副都心線}} == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷}} - {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} - {{ill|Shibuya Ward Office|ja|渋谷区役所}} * {{ill|Shinto|ja|神社}} - {{ill|Tokyo Shrine Association|ja|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|Deities in Shinto|ja|deity}} - {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} - {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} * {{ill|Spain-zaka|ja|スペイン坂}} * {{ill|Cultural Village Street|ja|文化村通り}} * {{ill|Dogenzaka|ja|道玄坂}} * {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/shibuya/2065/|Kitaya Inari Shrine}} (Tokyo Shrine Association) Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}}, {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18年}} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in {{ill|September|ja|9月}}) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3年}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|ja|1810年}} and {{ill|1830|ja|1830年}}){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Bunmei era (1469–1486)|ja|文明}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|ja|1997年}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kikutake Kiyonori|ja|菊竹清訓}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}}) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}) * {{ill|Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命}} (a deity of {{ill|longevity|ja|長寿}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|ja|NHK放送センター}} and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42年}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|ja|1920年}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} * {{ill|Udagawachō|ja|宇田川町}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅}} (major terminal station) : Served by: * {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}: {{ill|Yamanote Line|ja|山手線}}, {{ill|Saikyō Line|ja|埼京線}}, {{ill|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line|ja|湘南新宿ライン}}, {{ill|Narita Express|ja|成田エクスプレス}} * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄}}: {{ill|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|ja|東急東横線}}, {{ill|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|ja|東急田園都市線}} * {{ill|Keiō Corporation|ja|京王電鉄}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ}}: {{ill|Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}}, {{ill|Hanzōmon Line|ja|東京メトロ半蔵門線}}, {{ill|Fukutoshin Line|ja|東京メトロ副都心線}} == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}}== See Also == * {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷}} - {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} - {{ill|Shibuya Ward Office|ja|渋谷区役所}} * {{ill|Shinto|ja|神社}} - {{ill|Tokyo Shrine Association|ja|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|Deities in Shinto|ja|deity}} - {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} - {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} * {{ill|Spain-zaka|ja|スペイン坂}} * {{ill|Cultural Village Street|ja|文化村通り}} * {{ill|Dogenzaka|ja|道玄坂}} * {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/shibuya/2065/|Kitaya Inari Shrine}} (Tokyo Shrine Association)
