Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}}, {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18年}} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in {{ill|September|ja|9月}}) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3年}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|ja|1810年}} and {{ill|1830|ja|1830年}}){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Bunmei era (1469–1486)|ja|文明}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|ja|1997年}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kikutake Kiyonori|ja|菊竹清訓}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}}) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}) * {{ill|Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命}} (a deity of {{ill|longevity|ja|長寿}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|ja|NHK放送センター}} and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42年}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|ja|1920年}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} * {{ill|Udagawachō|ja|宇田川町}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅}} (major terminal station) : Served by: * {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}: {{ill|Yamanote Line|ja|山手線}}, {{ill|Saikyō Line|ja|埼京線}}, {{ill|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line|ja|湘南新宿ライン}}, {{ill|Narita Express|ja|成田エクスプレス}} * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄}}: {{ill|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|ja|東急東横線}}, {{ill|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|ja|東急田園都市線}} * {{ill|Keiō Corporation|ja|京王電鉄}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ}}: {{ill|Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}}, {{ill|Hanzōmon Line|ja|東京メトロ半蔵門線}}, {{ill|Fukutoshin Line|ja|東京メトロ副都心線}} == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == See Also! == * {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷}} - {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} - {{ill|Shibuya Ward Office|ja|渋谷区役所}} * {{ill|Shinto|ja|神社}} - {{ill|Tokyo Shrine Association|ja|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|Deities in Shinto|ja|deity}} - {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} - {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} * {{ill|Spain-zaka|ja|スペイン坂}} * {{ill|Cultural Village Street|ja|文化村通り}} * {{ill|Dogenzaka|ja|道玄坂}} * {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/shibuya/2065/|Kitaya Inari Shrine}} (Tokyo Shrine Association) The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|ja|NHK放送センター}} and Shibuya Ward Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}}, {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18年}} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in {{ill|September|ja|9月}}) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3年}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|ja|1810年}} and {{ill|1830|ja|1830年}}){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Bunmei era (1469–1486)|ja|文明}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|ja|1997年}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kikutake Kiyonori|ja|菊竹清訓}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}}) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}) * {{ill|Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命}} (a deity of {{ill|longevity|ja|長寿}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|ja|NHK放送センター}} and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42nd year }} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|ja|1920年}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} * {{ill|Udagawachō|ja|宇田川町}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅}} (major terminal station) : Served by: * {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}: {{ill|Yamanote Line|ja|山手線}}, {{ill|Saikyō Line|ja|埼京線}}, {{ill|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line|ja|湘南新宿ライン}}, {{ill|Narita Express|ja|成田エクスプレス}} * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄}}: {{ill|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|ja|東急東横線}}, {{ill|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|ja|東急田園都市線}} * {{ill|Keiō Corporation|ja|京王電鉄}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ}}: {{ill|Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}}, {{ill|Hanzōmon Line|ja|東京メトロ半蔵門線}}, {{ill|Fukutoshin Line|ja|東京メトロ副都心線}} == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} - {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} * {{ill|Spain-zaka|ja|スペイン坂}} * {{ill|Cultural Village Street|ja|文化村通り}} * {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}}, {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18年}} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in {{ill|September|ja|9月}}) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3rd year }} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|ja|1810年}} and {{ill|1830|ja|1830年}}){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Bunmei era (1469–1486)|ja|文明}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|ja|1997th year }} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kikutake Kiyonori|ja|菊竹清訓}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}}) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}) * {{ill|Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命}} (a deity of {{ill|longevity|ja|長寿}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|ja|NHK放送センター}} and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42nd year }} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|ja|1920年}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} * {{ill|Udagawachō|ja|宇田川町}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅}} (major terminal station) : Served by: * {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}: {{ill|Yamanote Line|ja|山手線}}, {{ill|Saikyō Line|ja|埼京線}}, {{ill|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line|ja|湘南新宿ライン}}, {{ill|Narita Express|ja|成田エクスプレス}} * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄}}: {{ill|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|ja|東急東横線}}, {{ill|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|ja|東急田園都市線}} * {{ill|Keiō Corporation|ja|京王電鉄}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ}}: {{ill|Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}}, {{ill|Hanzōmon Line|ja|東京メトロ半蔵門線}}, {{ill|Fukutoshin Line|ja|東京メトロ副都心線}} == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} - {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} * {{ill|Spain-zaka|ja|スペイン坂}} * {{ill|Cultural Village Street|ja|文化村通り}} * Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}}, {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18th year }} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in {{ill|September|ja|9月}}) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3rd year }} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|ja|1810th year }} and {{ill|1830|ja|1830th year }}){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Bunmei era (1469–1486)|ja|文明}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|ja|1997th year }} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kikutake Kiyonori|ja|菊竹清訓}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}}) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}) * {{ill|Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命}} (a deity of {{ill|longevity|ja|長寿}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|ja|NHK放送センター}} and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42nd year }} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|ja|1920th year }} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} * {{ill|Udagawachō|ja|宇田川町}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅}} (major terminal station) : Served by: * {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}: {{ill|Yamanote Line|ja|山手線}}, {{ill|Saikyō Line|ja|埼京線}}, {{ill|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line|ja|湘南新宿ライン}}, {{ill|Narita Express|ja|成田エクスプレス}} * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄}}: {{ill|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|ja|東急東横線}}, {{ill|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|ja|東急田園都市線}} * {{ill|Keiō Corporation|ja|京王電鉄}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ}}: {{ill|Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}}, {{ill|Hanzōmon Line|ja|東京メトロ半蔵門線}}, {{ill|Fukutoshin Line|ja|東京メトロ副都心線}} == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷}} - {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} - {{ill|Shibuya Ward Office|ja|渋谷区役所}} * {{ill|Shinto|ja|神社}} - {{ill|Tokyo Shrine Association|ja|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|Deities in Shinto|ja|deity}} - {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} - {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} * {{ill|Spain-zaka|ja|スペイン坂}} * {{ill|Cultural Village Street|ja|文化村通り}} * {{ill|Dogenzaka|ja|道玄坂}} * {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/shibuya/2065/|Kitaya Inari Shrine}} (Tokyo Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Kitadani Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kitadani Inari Shrine]][[Category:11]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Kitaya Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-01.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}}, {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18th year }} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in {{ill|September|ja|9月}}) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3rd year }} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|ja|1810th year }} and {{ill|1830|ja|1830th year }}){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Bunmei era (1469–1486)|ja|文明}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|ja|1997th year }} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kikutake Kiyonori|ja|菊竹清訓}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}}) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}) * {{ill|Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命}} (a deity of {{ill|longevity|ja|長寿}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|ja|NHK放送センター}} and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42nd year }} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|ja|1920th year }} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} * {{ill|Udagawachō|ja|宇田川町}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅}} (major terminal station) : Served by: * {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}: {{ill|Yamanote Line|ja|山手線}}, {{ill|Saikyō Line|ja|埼京線}}, {{ill|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line|ja|湘南新宿ライン}}, {{ill|Narita Express|ja|成田エクスプレス}} * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄}}: {{ill|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|ja|東急東横線}}, {{ill|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|ja|東急田園都市線}} * {{ill|Keiō Corporation|ja|京王電鉄}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ}}: {{ill|Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}}, {{ill|Hanzōmon Line|ja|東京メトロ半蔵門線}}, {{ill|Fukutoshin Line|ja|東京メトロ副都心線}} == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷}} - {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} - {{ill|Shibuya Ward Office|ja|渋谷区役所}} * {{ill|Shinto|ja|神社}} - {{ill|Tokyo Shrine Association|ja|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|Deities in Shinto|ja|deity}} - {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} - {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} * {{ill|Spain-zaka|ja|スペイン坂}} * {{ill|Cultural Village Street|ja|文化村通り}} * {{ill|Dogenzaka|ja|道玄坂}} * {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/shibuya/2065/|Kitaya Inari Shrine}} (Tokyo Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Kitadani Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kitadani Inari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier {{Infobox religious building | name = Kitaya Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-01.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}}, {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18th year }} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in {{ill|September|ja|9月}}) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3rd year }} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|ja|1810th year }} and {{ill|1830|ja|1830th year }}){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Bunmei era (1469–1486)|ja|文明}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|ja|1997th year }} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kikutake Kiyonori|ja|菊竹清訓}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}}) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}) * {{ill|Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命}} (a deity of {{ill|longevity|ja|長寿}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|ja|NHK放送センター}} and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42nd year }} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|ja|1920th year }} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} * {{ill|Udagawachō|ja|宇田川町}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅}} (major terminal station) : Served by: * {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}: {{ill|Yamanote Line|ja|山手線}}, {{ill|Saikyō Line|ja|埼京線}}, {{ill|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line|ja|湘南新宿ライン}}, {{ill|Narita Express|ja|成田エクスプレス}} * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄}}: {{ill|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|ja|東急東横線}}, {{ill|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|ja|東急田園都市線}} * {{ill|Keiō Corporation|ja|京王電鉄}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ}}: {{ill|Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}}, {{ill|Hanzōmon Line|ja|東京メトロ半蔵門線}}, {{ill|Fukutoshin Line|ja|東京メトロ副都心線}} == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷}} - {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南}} - {{ill|Shibuya Ward Office|ja|渋谷区役所}} * {{ill|Shinto|ja|神社}} - {{ill|Tokyo Shrine Association|ja|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|Deities in Shinto|ja|deity}} - {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り}} - {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り}} * {{ill|Spain-zaka|ja|スペイン坂}} * {{ill|Cultural Village Street|ja|文化村通り}} * {{ill|Dogenzaka|ja|道玄坂}} * {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/shibuya/2065/|Kitaya Inari Shrine}} (Tokyo Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kitadani Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Kitadani Inari Shrine]] [[ja:北谷稲荷神社]] [[en:Kitadani Inari Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kitadani Inari Shrine}}

The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|ja|1997th year|12=draft}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kikutake Kiyonori|ja|菊竹清訓|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|12=draft}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|12=draft}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命|12=draft}} (a deity of {{ill|longevity|ja|長寿|12=draft}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能|12=draft}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|12=draft}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|12=draft}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|12=draft}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|ja|NHK放送センター|12=draft}} and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42nd year|12=draft}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|ja|1920th year|12=draft}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川|12=draft}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南|12=draft}} * {{ill|Udagawachō|ja|宇田川町|12=draft}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目|12=draft}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目|12=draft}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目|12=draft}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り|12=draft}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅|12=draft}} (major terminal station) : Served by: * {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}}: {{ill|Yamanote Line|ja|山手線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Saikyō Line|ja|埼京線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line|ja|湘南新宿ライン|12=draft}}, {{ill|Narita Express|ja|成田エクスプレス|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄|12=draft}}: {{ill|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|ja|東急東横線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|ja|東急田園都市線|12=draft}} * {{ill|Keiō Corporation|ja|京王電鉄|12=draft}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ|12=draft}}: {{ill|Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hanzōmon Line|ja|東京メトロ半蔵門線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukutoshin Line|ja|東京メトロ副都心線|12=draft}} == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷|12=draft}} - {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南|12=draft}} - {{ill|Shibuya Ward Office|ja|渋谷区役所|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shinto|ja|神社|12=draft}} - {{ill|Tokyo Shrine Association|ja|東京都神社庁|12=draft}} * {{ill|Deities in Shinto|ja|deity|12=draft}} - {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|12=draft}} - {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|12=draft}} * {{ill|Spain-zaka|ja|スペイン坂|12=draft}} * {{ill|Cultural Village Street|ja|文化村通り|12=draft}} * {{ill|Dogenzaka|ja|道玄坂|12=draft}} * {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/shibuya/2065/|Kitaya Inari Shrine}} (Tokyo Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kitadani Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Kitadani Inari Shrine]] [[ja:北谷稲荷神社]] [[en:Kitadani Inari Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kitadani Inari Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kitaya Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-01.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神|12=draft}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|12=draft}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年|12=draft}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18th year|12=draft}} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in {{ill|September|ja|9月|12=draft}}) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|12=draft}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3rd year|12=draft}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=draft}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|ja|1810th year|12=draft}} and {{ill|1830|ja|1830th year|12=draft}}){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Bunmei era (1469–1486)|ja|文明|12=draft}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|ja|1997th year|12=draft}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kikutake Kiyonori|ja|菊竹清訓|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|12=draft}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|12=draft}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命|12=draft}} (a deity of {{ill|longevity|ja|長寿|12=draft}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能|12=draft}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|12=draft}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|12=draft}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|12=draft}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|ja|NHK放送センター|12=draft}} and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42nd year|12=draft}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|ja|1920th year|12=draft}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川|12=draft}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南|12=draft}} * {{ill|Udagawachō|ja|宇田川町|12=draft}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目|12=draft}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目|12=draft}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目|12=draft}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り|12=draft}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅|12=draft}} (major terminal station) : Served by: * {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}}: {{ill|Yamanote Line|ja|山手線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Saikyō Line|ja|埼京線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line|ja|湘南新宿ライン|12=draft}}, {{ill|Narita Express|ja|成田エクスプレス|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄|12=draft}}: {{ill|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|ja|東急東横線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|ja|東急田園都市線|12=draft}} * {{ill|Keiō Corporation|ja|京王電鉄|12=draft}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ|12=draft}}: {{ill|Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hanzōmon Line|ja|東京メトロ半蔵門線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukutoshin Line|ja|東京メトロ副都心線|12=draft}} == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷|12=draft}} - {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南|12=draft}} - {{ill|Shibuya Ward Office|ja|渋谷区役所|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shinto|ja|神社|12=draft}} - {{ill|Tokyo Shrine Association|ja|東京都神社庁|12=draft}} * {{ill|Deities in Shinto|ja|deity|12=draft}} - {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|12=draft}} - {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|12=draft}} * {{ill|Spain-zaka|ja|スペイン坂|12=draft}} * {{ill|Cultural Village Street|ja|文化村通り|12=draft}} * {{ill|Dogenzaka|ja|道玄坂|12=draft}} * {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/shibuya/2065/|Kitaya Inari Shrine}} (Tokyo Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kitadani Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Kitadani Inari Shrine]] [[ja:北谷稲荷神社]] [[en:Kitadani Inari Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kitadani Inari Shrine}}

{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神|12=draft}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|12=draft}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年|12=draft}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18th year|12=draft}} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in {{ill|September|ja|9月|12=draft}}) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|12=draft}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3rd year|12=draft}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=draft}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|ja|1810th year|12=draft}} and {{ill|1830|ja|1830th year|12=draft}}){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Bunmei era (1469–1486)|ja|文明|12=draft}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|ja|1997th year|12=draft}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kikutake Kiyonori|ja|菊竹清訓|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|12=draft}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|12=draft}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命|12=draft}} (a deity of {{ill|longevity|ja|長寿|12=draft}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能|12=draft}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|12=draft}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|12=draft}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|12=draft}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|ja|NHK放送センター|12=draft}} and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42nd year|12=draft}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|ja|1920th year|12=draft}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川|12=draft}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南|12=draft}} * {{ill|Udagawachō|ja|宇田川町|12=draft}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目|12=draft}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目|12=draft}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目|12=draft}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り|12=draft}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅|12=draft}} (major terminal station) : Served by: * {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}}: {{ill|Yamanote Line|ja|山手線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Saikyō Line|ja|埼京線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line|ja|湘南新宿ライン|12=draft}}, {{ill|Narita Express|ja|成田エクスプレス|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄|12=draft}}: {{ill|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|ja|東急東横線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|ja|東急田園都市線|12=draft}} * {{ill|Keiō Corporation|ja|京王電鉄|12=draft}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ|12=draft}}: {{ill|Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hanzōmon Line|ja|東京メトロ半蔵門線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukutoshin Line|ja|東京メトロ副都心線|12=draft}} == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷|12=draft}} - {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南|12=draft}} - {{ill|Shibuya Ward Office|ja|渋谷区役所|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shinto|ja|神社|12=draft}} - {{ill|Tokyo Shrine Association|ja|東京都神社庁|12=draft}} * {{ill|Deities in Shinto|ja|deity|12=draft}} - {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|12=draft}} - {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|12=draft}} * {{ill|Spain-zaka|ja|スペイン坂|12=draft}} * {{ill|Cultural Village Street|ja|文化村通り|12=draft}} * {{ill|Dogenzaka|ja|道玄坂|12=draft}} * {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/shibuya/2065/|Kitaya Inari Shrine}} (Tokyo Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kitadani Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Kitadani Inari Shrine]] [[ja:北谷稲荷神社]] [[en:Kitadani Inari Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kitadani Inari Shrine}}

Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神|12=draft}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|12=draft}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年|12=draft}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18th year|12=draft}} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in {{ill|September|ja|9月|12=draft}}) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|12=draft}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3rd year|12=draft}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=draft}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|ja|1810th year|12=draft}} and {{ill|1830|ja|1830th year|12=draft}}){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Bunmei era (1469–1486)|ja|文明|12=draft}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|ja|1997th year|12=draft}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kikutake Kiyonori|ja|菊竹清訓|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|12=draft}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|12=draft}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命|12=draft}} (a deity of {{ill|longevity|ja|長寿|12=draft}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能|12=draft}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|12=draft}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|12=draft}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|12=draft}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|ja|NHK放送センター|12=draft}} and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42nd year|12=draft}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|ja|1920th year|12=draft}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川|12=draft}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南|12=draft}} * {{ill|Udagawachō|ja|宇田川町|12=draft}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目|12=draft}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目|12=draft}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目|12=draft}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り|12=draft}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅|12=draft}} (major terminal station) : Served by: * {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}}: {{ill|Yamanote Line|ja|山手線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Saikyō Line|ja|埼京線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line|ja|湘南新宿ライン|12=draft}}, {{ill|Narita Express|ja|成田エクスプレス|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄|12=draft}}: {{ill|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|ja|東急東横線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|ja|東急田園都市線|12=draft}} * {{ill|Keiō Corporation|ja|京王電鉄|12=draft}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ|12=draft}}: {{ill|Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hanzōmon Line|ja|東京メトロ半蔵門線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukutoshin Line|ja|東京メトロ副都心線|12=draft}} == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷|12=draft}} - {{ill|Jinnan|ja|神南|12=draft}} - {{ill|Shibuya Ward Office|ja|渋谷区役所|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shinto|ja|神社|12=draft}} - {{ill|Tokyo Shrine Association|ja|東京都神社庁|12=draft}} * {{ill|Deities in Shinto|ja|deity|12=draft}} - {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|稲荷神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|12=draft}} - {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|12=draft}} * {{ill|Spain-zaka|ja|スペイン坂|12=draft}} * {{ill|Cultural Village Street|ja|文化村通り|12=draft}} * {{ill|Dogenzaka|ja|道玄坂|12=draft}} * {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/shibuya/2065/|Kitaya Inari Shrine}} (Tokyo Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kitadani Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kitadani Inari Shrine]] [[ja:北谷稲荷神社]] [[en:Kitadani Inari Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kitadani Inari Shrine}}

revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称=北谷稲荷神社 | 画像=[[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-01.jpg|250px]]
拝殿
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 39 | 緯度秒 = 56.2 | 経度度 = 139 | 経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 1.4 | 所在地=[[東京都]][[渋谷区]][[神南#神南一丁目|神南一丁目]]4番1号 | 祭神=宇迦之霊大神・大己貴大神・大宮比賣大神・神功皇后・大田大神 | 社格=[[村社]] | 創建=推定 [[1469年]]（[[文明 (日本)|文明]]元年） - [[1486年]]（文明18年）頃より以前 | 例祭=北谷稲荷神社秋季例大祭　（毎年[[9月]]） }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|鳥居]] '''北谷稲荷神社'''（きたやいなりじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[渋谷区]]神南一丁目に鎮座する[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == 正確な創建年は不明である。ただし、[[1660年]]（[[万治]]3年）に大破して再建した際の[[棟札]]や、[[武蔵国]]の[[地誌]]「[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]（[[1810年]]起稿 - [[1830年]]完成）」に{{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=巻ノ10豊島郡ノ2|chapter=上渋谷村|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}}「[[文明 (日本)|文明年間]]（[[1469年]] – [[1486年]]）、[[駿河国|駿河]]より移りし時其の邸内の[[艮]]（ごん、[[八卦]]の方角で東北）の方に[[勧請]]（かんじょう、[[祭神]]の[[分霊]]）せり」などの記述があることから、早ければ[[1469年]]（[[文明 (日本)|文明]]元年）頃には現在の場所に創建されていたと推定される。[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html 東京都神社庁 Official Web Site「渋谷区の神社」]より。 [[1997年]]、[[菊竹清訓]]建築設計事務所による[[デザイン]]で竣工し、現在のモダンな外観となった。 == 祭神 == [[祭神]]として祀る主な大神は下記の通り。 *宇迦之霊大神（うかのみたまのおおかみ）　⇒　[[稲荷神]]の[[宇迦之御魂神]]。 *大己貴大神（おおなむちのおおかみ）　⇒　[[大国主]]の別称。 *大宮比賣大神（おおみやひめのおおかみ）　⇒　[[大宮能売神]]。 *[[神功皇后]]（じんぐうこうごう）　⇒　[[仲哀天皇]]の[[皇后]]。 *[[太田命|大田大神]]（おおたのかみ）　⇒　[[芸能]]の[[長寿]]の神。 == 特徴 == *立地としては、西側の[[渋谷公園通り]]と東側の[[ファイヤー通り]]に挟まれており、近隣は[[NHK放送センター]]や[[渋谷区役所]]などの[[公共施設]]、[[ファッション]]関連の[[商業施設]]などが数多く建ち並ぶ[[繁華街]]となっている。[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html#contents 渋谷公園通りTOWN MAP ファイアー通り方面]{{リンク切れ|date=2019-10-15}}より。 *境内には、[[1909年]]（[[明治]]42年）に練兵場を設置した際に農民が建てた[[石碑]]や、[[1920年]]（[[大正]]9年）の[[宇田川 (東京都)|宇田川]]の[[洪水]]後に建てられた[[治水]][[記念碑]]が残されている。[[明治]]時代の現地一帯は[[陸軍省]]が管理する練兵場が広がっており、代々木練兵場や青山練兵場などがあった。 == 氏子地域 == * 渋谷区[[神南]] * 渋谷区[[宇田川町]]1～13、14・15 * 渋谷区[[渋谷]]一丁目21～23 * 渋谷区[[神宮前 (渋谷区)|神宮前]]六丁目12（一部）、14（一部）、5～19、21～26、27（一部） * 渋谷区神宮前一丁目7～9、14（一部）、16・17・19・20（いわゆる「[[原宿]]の[[竹下通り]]」一帯） == 最寄駅 == ;[[ターミナル駅]]「[[渋谷駅]]」 :[[東日本旅客鉄道]]（JR東日本）「渋谷駅」　⇒　[[山手線]]・[[埼京線]]・[[湘南新宿ライン]]・[[成田エクスプレス]] :[[東急電鉄]]（東急）「渋谷駅」 ⇒　[[東急東横線]]（東横線）・[[東急田園都市線]] :[[京王電鉄]]（京王）「渋谷駅」　⇒　[[井の頭線]] :[[東京地下鉄]]（東京メトロ）「渋谷駅」　⇒　[[東京メトロ銀座線|銀座線]]・[[東京メトロ半蔵門線|半蔵門線]]・[[東京メトロ副都心線|副都心線]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[渋谷]] - [[東京都]][[渋谷区]][[神南]] - [[渋谷区役所]] *[[神社]] - [[東京都神社庁]] *[[祭神]] - [[稲荷神]] *[[渋谷公園通り]] - [[プチ公園通り]] *[[ファイヤー通り]] *[[渋谷センター街]] *[[スペイン坂]] *[[文化村通り]]（Bunkamura通り） *[[道玄坂]] *[[間坂]] *[[宮益坂]] == 外部リンク == * {{Official website|http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/shibuya/2065/|name=北谷稲荷神社}} - 東京都神社庁のページ {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{渋谷区の町名}} {{デフォルトソート:きたやいなりしんしや}} [[Category:渋谷区の神社]] [[Category:東京都の稲荷神社]] [[Category:渋谷]] [[Category:東京都の旧村社]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Kitaya Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-01.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1469|qq=}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1486|qq=}} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in [[:en:September|September]]) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munafuda|qq=}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1660|qq=}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|qq=}} (compiled between [[:en:1810|1810]] and [[:en:1830|1830]]){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era (1469–1486)]]. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:1997|1997]] with a modern design by the architectural firm led by [[:en:Kiyonori Kikutake|Kikutake Kiyonori]]. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} (associated with [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (another name for [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]) * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omiyanome|qq=}} * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (consort of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a deity of [[:en:Longevity|longevity]] and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arts|qq=}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|qq=}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire-dori Avenue|qq=}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/commercial district|qq=}} with nearby landmarks such as the [[:en:NHK Broadcasting Center|NHK Broadcasting Center]] and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the [[:en:1920|1920]] flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udagawa River|qq=}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * [[:en:Jinnan, Shibuya|Jinnan]] * [[:en:Udagawachō|Udagawachō]] (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya 1-chome|qq=}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 6-chome|qq=}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 1-chome|qq=}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshita Street|qq=}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; [[:en:Shibuya Station|Shibuya Station]] (major terminal station) : Served by: * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]: [[:en:Yamanote Line|Yamanote Line]], [[:en:Saikyō Line|Saikyō Line]], [[:en:Shōnan–Shinjuku Line|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line]], [[:en:Narita Express|Narita Express]] * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyū Corporation|qq=}}: [[:en:Tōyoko Line|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]], [[:en:Den-en-toshi Line|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line]] * [[:en:Keio Corporation|Keiō Corporation]]: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō Inokashira Line|qq=}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metro|qq=}}: [[:en:Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|Ginza Line]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro Hanzōmon Line|Hanzōmon Line]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro Fukutoshin Line|Fukutoshin Line]] == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Shibuya (district)|Shibuya]] - [[:en:Jinnan, Shibuya|Jinnan]] - {{ill|Shibuya Ward Office|ja|渋谷区役所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Ward Office|qq=}} * [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto]] - {{ill|Tokyo Shrine Association|ja|東京都神社庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Shrine Association|qq=}} * {{ill|Deities in Shinto|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deities in Shinto|qq=}} - [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]] * {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|qq=}} - {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire-dori Avenue|qq=}} * {{ill|Spain-zaka|ja|スペイン坂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spain-zaka|qq=}} * {{ill|Cultural Village Street|ja|文化村通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Village Street|qq=}} * [[:en:Dōgenzaka|Dogenzaka]] * {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/shibuya/2065/|Kitaya Inari Shrine}} (Tokyo Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|北谷稲荷神社|version=101570458|comment=Imported full ja history}} 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 1-chome|qq=}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/commercial district|qq=}} with nearby landmarks such as the [[:en:NHK Broadcasting Center|NHK Broadcasting Center]] and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the [[:en:1920|1920]] flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udagawa River|qq=}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * [[:en:Jinnan, Shibuya|Jinnan]] * [[:en:Udagawachō|Udagawachō]] (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya 1-chome|qq=}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 6-chome|qq=}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 1-chome|qq=}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshita Street|qq=}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; [[:en:Shibuya Station|Shibuya Station]] (major terminal station) : However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munafuda|qq=}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1660|qq=}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|qq=}} (compiled between [[:en:1810|1810]] and [[:en:1830|1830]]){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era (1469–1486)]]. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:1997|1997]] with a modern design by the architectural firm led by [[:en:Kiyonori Kikutake|Kikutake Kiyonori]]. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} (associated with [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (another name for [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]) * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omiyanome|qq=}} * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (consort of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a deity of [[:en:Longevity|longevity]] and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arts|qq=}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|qq=}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire-dori Avenue|qq=}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/commercial district|qq=}} with nearby landmarks such as the [[:en:NHK Broadcasting Center|NHK {{Infobox religious building | name = Kitaya Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-01.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1469|qq=}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1486|qq=}} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in [[:en:September|September]]) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munafuda|qq=}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1660|qq=}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|qq=}} (compiled between [[:en:1810|1810]] and [[:en:1830|1830]]){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era (1469–1486)]]. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:1997|1997]] with a modern design by the architectural firm led by [[:en:Kiyonori Kikutake|Kikutake Kiyonori]]. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} (associated with [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (another name for [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]) * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omiyanome|qq=}} * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (consort of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a deity of [[:en:Longevity|longevity]] and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arts|qq=}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|qq=}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire-dori Avenue|qq=}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/commercial district|qq=}} with nearby landmarks such as the [[:en:NHK Broadcasting Center|NHK Broadcasting Center]] and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the [[:en:1920|1920]] flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udagawa River|qq=}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * [[:en:Jinnan, Shibuya|Jinnan]] * [[:en:Udagawachō|Udagawachō]] (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya 1-chome|qq=}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 6-chome|qq=}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 1-chome|qq=}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshita Street|qq=}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; [[:en:Shibuya Station|Shibuya Station]] (major terminal station) : Express|Narita Express]] * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyū Corporation|qq=}}: [[:en:Tōyoko Line|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]], [[:en:Den-en-toshi Line|Tōkyū {{Infobox religious building | name = Kitaya Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-01.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1469|qq=}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1486|qq=}} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in [[:en:September|September]]) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munafuda|qq=}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1660|qq=}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|qq=}} (compiled between [[:en:1810|1810]] and [[:en:1830|1830]]){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era (1469–1486)]]. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:1997|1997]] with a modern design by the architectural firm led by [[:en:Kiyonori Kikutake|Kikutake Kiyonori]]. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} (associated with [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (another name for [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]) * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omiyanome|qq=}} * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (consort of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a deity of [[:en:Longevity|longevity]] and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arts|qq=}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|qq=}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire-dori Avenue|qq=}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/commercial district|qq=}} with nearby landmarks such as the [[:en:NHK Broadcasting Center|NHK Broadcasting Center]] and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the [[:en:1920|1920]] flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udagawa River|qq=}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * [[:en:Jinnan, Shibuya|Jinnan]] * [[:en:Udagawachō|Udagawachō]] (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya 1-chome|qq=}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 6-chome|qq=}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 1-chome|qq=}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshita Street|qq=}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; [[:en:Shibuya Station|Shibuya Station]] (major terminal station) : Served by: * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]: [[:en:Yamanote Line|Yamanote Line]], [[:en:Saikyō Line|Saikyō Line]], [[:en:Shōnan–Shinjuku Line|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line]], [[:en:Narita Express|Narita Express]] * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyū Corporation|qq=}}: [[:en:Tōyoko Line|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]], [[:en:Den-en-toshi Line|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line]] * [[:en:Keio Corporation|Keiō Corporation]]: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō Inokashira Line|qq=}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metro|qq=}}: [[:en:Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|Ginza Line]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro Hanzōmon Line|Hanzōmon Line]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro Fukutoshin Line|Fukutoshin Line]] == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Shibuya (district)|Shibuya]] - [[:en:Jinnan, Shibuya|Jinnan]] - {{ill|Shibuya Ward Office|ja|渋谷区役所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Ward Office|qq=}} * [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto]] - {{ill|Tokyo Shrine Association|ja|東京都神社庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Shrine Association|qq=}} * {{ill|Deities in Shinto|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deities in Shinto|qq=}} - [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]] * {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|qq=}} - {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire-dori Avenue|qq=}} * {{ill|Spain-zaka|ja|スペイン坂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spain-zaka|qq=}} * {{ill|Cultural Village Street|ja|文化村通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Village Street|qq=}} * [[:en:Dōgenzaka|Dogenzaka]] * {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/shibuya/2065/|Kitaya Inari Shrine}} (Tokyo Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|北谷稲荷神社|version=101570458|comment=Imported full ja history}}
