{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}, [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1469|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1486|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in [[:en:September|September]]) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munafuda|qq=}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1660|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|qq=}} (compiled between [[:en:1810|1810]] and [[:en:1830|1830]]){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era (1469–1486)]]. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:1997|1997]] with a modern design by the architectural firm led by [[:en:Kiyonori Kikutake|Kikutake Kiyonori]]. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (associated with [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (another name for [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]) * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omiyanome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}} * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (consort of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a deity of [[:en:Longevity|longevity]] and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arts|qq=}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|qq=}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire-dori Avenue|qq=}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/commercial district|qq=}} with nearby landmarks such as the [[:en:NHK Broadcasting Center|NHK Broadcasting Center]] and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1909年}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the [[:en:1920|1920]] flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udagawa River|qq=}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * [[:en:Jinnan, Shibuya|Jinnan]] * [[:en:Udagawachō|Udagawachō]] (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya 1-chome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}, [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | founded = Estimated between {{ill|1469|ja|文明元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1469|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1486|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in [[:en:September|September]]) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munafuda|qq=}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1660|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|qq=}} (compiled between [[:en:1810|1810]] and [[:en:1830|1830]]){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era (1469–1486)]]. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:1997|1997]] with a modern design by the architectural firm led by [[:en:Kiyonori Kikutake|Kikutake Kiyonori]]. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (associated with [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (another name for [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]) * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omiyanome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}} * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (consort of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a deity of [[:en:Longevity|longevity]] and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arts|qq=}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|qq=}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire-dori Avenue|qq=}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/commercial district|qq=}} with nearby landmarks such as the [[:en:NHK Broadcasting Center|NHK Broadcasting Center]] and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|ja|明治42年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1909年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the [[:en:1920|1920]] flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udagawa River|qq=}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * [[:en:Jinnan, Shibuya|Jinnan]] * [[:en:Udagawachō|Udagawachō]] (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya 1-chome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 6-chome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 1-chome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshita Street|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; [[:en:Shibuya Station|Shibuya Station]] (major terminal station) : Served by: * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]: [[:en:Yamanote Line|Yamanote Line]], [[:en:Saikyō Line|Saikyō Line]], [[:en:Shōnan–Shinjuku Line|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line]], However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munafuda|qq=}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1660|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|qq=}} (compiled between [[:en:1810|1810]] and [[:en:1830|1830]]){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era (1469–1486)]]. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:1997|1997]] with a modern design by the architectural firm led by [[:en:Kiyonori Kikutake|Kikutake Kiyonori]]. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (associated with [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (another name for [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]) * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omiyanome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}} * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (consort of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a deity of [[:en:Longevity|longevity]] and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arts|qq=}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|qq=}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire-dori Avenue|qq=}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/commercial district|qq=}} with nearby landmarks such as the [[:en:NHK Broadcasting Center|NHK Broadcasting Center]] and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in [[:en:1909|Meiji 42]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the [[:en:1920|1920]] flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udagawa River|qq=}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * [[:en:Jinnan, Shibuya|Jinnan]] * [[:en:Udagawachō|Udagawachō]] (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya 1-chome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 6-chome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 1-chome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshita Street|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; [[:en:Shibuya Station|Shibuya Station]] (major terminal station) : Served by: * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]: [[:en:Yamanote Line|Yamanote Line]], [[:en:Saikyō Line|Saikyō Line]], [[:en:Shōnan–Shinjuku Line|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line]], [[:en:Narita Express|Narita Express]] * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyū Corporation|qq=}}: [[:en:Tōyoko Line|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]], [[:en:Den-en-toshi Line|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line]] * [[:en:Keio Corporation|Keiō Corporation]]: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō Inokashira Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京王井の頭線|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京王井の頭線}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京地下鉄|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京地下鉄}}: [[:en:Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|Ginza Line]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro Hanzōmon Line|Hanzōmon Line]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro Fukutoshin Line|Fukutoshin Shrine Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁}} * {{ill|Deities in Shinto|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deities in {{Infobox religious building | name = Kitaya Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-01.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}, [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | founded = Estimated between [[:en:1469|1469]] and {{ill|1486|ja|文明18年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1486|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in [[:en:September|September]]) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munafuda|qq=}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|ja|万治3年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1660|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|qq=}} (compiled between [[:en:1810|1810]] and [[:en:1830|1830]]){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era (1469–1486)]]. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:1997|1997]] with a modern design by the architectural firm led by [[:en:Kiyonori Kikutake|Kikutake Kiyonori]]. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (associated with [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (another name for [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]) * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omiyanome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}} * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (consort of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a deity of [[:en:Longevity|longevity]] and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arts|qq=}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|qq=}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire-dori Avenue|qq=}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/commercial district|qq=}} with nearby landmarks such as the [[:en:NHK Broadcasting Center|NHK Broadcasting Center]] and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in [[:en:1909|Meiji 42]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the [[:en:1920|1920]] flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udagawa River|qq=}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * [[:en:Jinnan, Shibuya|Jinnan]] * [[:en:Udagawachō|Udagawachō]] (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya 1-chome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 6-chome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 1-chome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshita Street|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; [[:en:Shibuya Station|Shibuya Station]] (major terminal station) : Served by: * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]: [[:en:Yamanote Line|Yamanote Line]], [[:en:Saikyō Line|Saikyō Line]], [[:en:Shōnan–Shinjuku Line|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line]], [[:en:Narita Express|Narita Express]] * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyū Corporation|qq=}}: [[:en:Tōyoko Line|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]], [[:en:Den-en-toshi Line|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line]] * [[:en:Keio Corporation|Keiō Corporation]]: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō Inokashira Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京王井の頭線|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京王井の頭線}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京地下鉄|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京地下鉄}}: [[:en:Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|Ginza Line]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro Hanzōmon Line|Hanzōmon Line]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro Fukutoshin Line|Fukutoshin {{Infobox religious building | name = Kitaya Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-01.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}, [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | founded = Estimated between [[:en:1469|1469]] and [[:en:1486|1486]] or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in [[:en:September|September]]) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munafuda|qq=}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in [[:en:1660|1660]] and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|qq=}} (compiled between [[:en:1810|1810]] and [[:en:1830|1830]]){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era (1469–1486)]]. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:1997|1997]] with a modern design by the architectural firm led by [[:en:Kiyonori Kikutake|Kikutake Kiyonori]]. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (associated with [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (another name for [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]) * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omiyanome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}} * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (consort of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a deity of [[:en:Longevity|longevity]] and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arts|qq=}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|qq=}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire-dori Avenue|qq=}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/commercial district|qq=}} with nearby landmarks such as the [[:en:NHK Broadcasting Center|NHK Broadcasting Center]] and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in [[:en:1909|Meiji 42]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the [[:en:1920|1920]] flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udagawa River|qq=}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * [[:en:Jinnan, Shibuya|Jinnan]] * [[:en:Udagawachō|Udagawachō]] (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya 1-chome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 6-chome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 1-chome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshita Street|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; [[:en:Shibuya Station|Shibuya Station]] (major terminal station) : Served by: * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]: [[:en:Yamanote Line|Yamanote Line]], [[:en:Saikyō Line|Saikyō Line]], [[:en:Shōnan–Shinjuku Line|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line]], [[:en:Narita Express|Narita Express]] * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyū Corporation|qq=}}: [[:en:Tōyoko Line|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]], [[:en:Den-en-toshi Line|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line]] * [[:en:Keio Corporation|Keiō Corporation]]: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō Inokashira Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京王井の頭線|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京王井の頭線}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京地下鉄|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京地下鉄}}: [[:en:Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|Ginza Line]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro Hanzōmon Line|Hanzōmon Line]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro Fukutoshin Line|Fukutoshin Line]] == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Shibuya (district)|Shibuya]] - [[:en:Jinnan, Shibuya|Jinnan]] - {{ill|Shibuya Ward Office|ja|渋谷区役所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Ward Office|qq=}} * [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto]] - {{ill|Tokyo Shrine Association|ja|東京都神社庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Shrine Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁}} * {{ill|Deities in Shinto|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deities in {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11404090]] Wikidata name: Kitaya Inari-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kitaya Inari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-01.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|width=250|height=|stroke-width=1}} | latitude = 35.6656 | longitude = 139.7004 | location = 4-1 Jinnan 1-chome, Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan | deity = {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}, [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | classification = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | founded = Estimated between [[:en:1469|1469]] and [[:en:1486|1486]] or earlier | festivals = Kitaya Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (annually in [[:en:September|September]]) }} [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo. == History == The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munafuda|qq=}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in [[:en:1660|1660]] and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|qq=}} (compiled between [[:en:1810|1810]] and [[:en:1830|1830]]){{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}} indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era (1469–1486)]]. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines] The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:1997|1997]] with a modern design by the architectural firm led by [[:en:Kiyonori Kikutake|Kikutake Kiyonori]]. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine honors the following main deities: * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (associated with [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (another name for [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]) * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omiyanome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}} * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (consort of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a deity of [[:en:Longevity|longevity]] and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arts|qq=}}) == Features == * The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|qq=}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire-dori Avenue|qq=}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/commercial district|qq=}} with nearby landmarks such as the [[:en:NHK Broadcasting Center|NHK Broadcasting Center]] and Shibuya Ward Office.[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map] * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in [[:en:1909|Meiji 42]] during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the [[:en:1920|1920]] flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udagawa River|qq=}}.During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds. == Parish District == The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * [[:en:Jinnan, Shibuya|Jinnan]] * [[:en:Udagawachō|Udagawachō]] (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya 1-chome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 6-chome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 1-chome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshita Street|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Harajuku) == Nearest Stations == ; [[:en:Shibuya Station|Shibuya Station]] (major terminal station) : Served by: * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]: [[:en:Yamanote Line|Yamanote Line]], [[:en:Saikyō Line|Saikyō Line]], [[:en:Shōnan–Shinjuku Line|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line]], [[:en:Narita Express|Narita Express]] * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyū Corporation|qq=}}: [[:en:Tōyoko Line|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]], [[:en:Den-en-toshi Line|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line]] * [[:en:Keio Corporation|Keiō Corporation]]: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō Inokashira Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京王井の頭線|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京王井の頭線}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京地下鉄|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京地下鉄}}: [[:en:Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|Ginza Line]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro Hanzōmon Line|Hanzōmon Line]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro Fukutoshin Line|Fukutoshin [[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo.
== History ==
The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munafuda|qq=}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in [[:en:1660|1660]] and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|qq=|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} (compiled between [[:en:1810|1810]] and [[:en:1830|1830]])<ref>{{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}}</ref> indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era (1469–1486)]]. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.<ref name="A">[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines]</ref>
The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:1997|1997]] with a modern design by the architectural firm led by [[:en:Kiyonori Kikutake|Kikutake Kiyonori]].
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine honors the following main deities:<ref name="A"></ref> * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (associated with [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (another name for [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]) * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omiyanome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}} * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (consort of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a deity of [[:en:Longevity|longevity]] and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arts|qq=|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}})
== Features ==
* The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|qq=|1=Shibuya Kōen-dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561683}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire-dori Avenue|qq=|1=Fire Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280335}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/commercial district|qq=|1=prosperous area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66364404}} with nearby landmarks such as the [[:en:NHK Broadcasting Center|NHK Broadcasting Center]] and Shibuya Ward Office.<ref>[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map]</ref> * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in [[:en:1909|Meiji 42]] during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the [[:en:1920|1920]] flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udagawa River|qq=|1=udagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449570}}.<ref group="note">During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds.</ref>
== Parish District ==
The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * [[:en:Jinnan, Shibuya|Jinnan]] * [[:en:Udagawachō|Udagawachō]] (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya 1-chome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 6-chome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 1-chome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshita Street|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Takeshita Street|WD=Q3179287}} in Harajuku)
== Nearest Stations ==
; [[:en:Shibuya Station|Shibuya Station]] (major terminal station) : Served by: * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]: [[:en:Yamanote Line|Yamanote Line]], [[:en:Saikyō Line|Saikyō Line]], [[:en:Shōnan–Shinjuku Line|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line]], [[:en:Narita Express|Narita Express]] * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyū Corporation|qq=|1=Tokyu Railways|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80229029}}: [[:en:Tōyoko Line|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]], [[:en:Den-en-toshi Line|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line]] * [[:en:Keio Corporation|Keiō Corporation]]: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō Inokashira Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京王井の頭線|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京王井の頭線|1=Keiō Inokashira Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191300}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京地下鉄|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京地下鉄|1=Tokyo Metro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682894}}: [[:en:Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|Ginza Line]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro Hanzōmon Line|Hanzōmon Line]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro Fukutoshin Line|Fukutoshin Line]]

[[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo.
== History ==
The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munafuda|qq=}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in [[:en:1660|1660]] and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|qq=|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} (compiled between [[:en:1810|1810]] and [[:en:1830|1830]])<ref>{{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}}</ref> indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era (1469–1486)]]. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.<ref name="A">[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines]</ref>

The shrine honors the following main deities:<ref name="A"></ref> * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (associated with [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (another name for [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]) * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omiyanome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}} * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (consort of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a deity of [[:en:Longevity|longevity]] and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arts|qq=|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}})
== Features ==
* The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|qq=|1=Shibuya Kōen-dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561683}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire-dori Avenue|qq=|1=Fire Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280335}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/commercial district|qq=|1=prosperous area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66364404}} with nearby landmarks such as the [[:en:NHK Broadcasting Center|NHK Broadcasting Center]] and Shibuya Ward Office.<ref>[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map]</ref> * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in [[:en:1909|Meiji 42]] during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the [[:en:1920|1920]] flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udagawa River|qq=|1=udagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449570}}.<ref group="note">During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds.</ref>

[[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo.
== History ==
The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munafuda|qq=}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in [[:en:1660|1660]] and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|qq=|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} (compiled between [[:en:1810|1810]] and [[:en:1830|1830]])<ref>{{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}}</ref> indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era (1469–1486)]]. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.<ref name="A">[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines]</ref>
The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:1997|1997]] with a modern design by the architectural firm led by [[:en:Kiyonori Kikutake|Kikutake Kiyonori]].
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine honors the following main deities:<ref name="A"></ref> * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (associated with [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (another name for [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]) * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omiyanome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}} * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (consort of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a deity of [[:en:Longevity|longevity]] and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arts|qq=|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}})
== Features ==
* The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|qq=|1=Shibuya Kōen-dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561683}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire-dori Avenue|qq=|1=Fire Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280335}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/commercial district|qq=|1=prosperous area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66364404}} with nearby landmarks such as the [[:en:NHK Broadcasting Center|NHK Broadcasting Center]] and Shibuya Ward Office.<ref>[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map]</ref> * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in [[:en:1909|Meiji 42]] during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the [[:en:1920|1920]] flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udagawa River|qq=|1=udagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449570}}.<ref group="note">During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds.</ref>
== Parish District ==
The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * [[:en:Jinnan, Shibuya|Jinnan]] * [[:en:Udagawachō|Udagawachō]] (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya 1-chome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 6-chome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 1-chome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshita Street|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Takeshita Street|WD=Q3179287}} in Harajuku)
== Nearest Stations ==

[[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo.
== History ==
The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munafuda|qq=}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in [[:en:1660|1660]] and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|qq=|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} (compiled between [[:en:1810|1810]] and [[:en:1830|1830]])<ref>{{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}}</ref> indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era (1469–1486)]]. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.<ref name="A">[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines]</ref>
The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:1997|1997]] with a modern design by the architectural firm led by [[:en:Kiyonori Kikutake|Kikutake Kiyonori]].
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine honors the following main deities:<ref name="A"></ref> * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (associated with [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (another name for [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]) * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omiyanome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}} * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (consort of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a deity of [[:en:Longevity|longevity]] and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arts|qq=|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}})
== Features ==
* The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|qq=|1=Shibuya Kōen-dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561683}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire-dori Avenue|qq=|1=Fire Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280335}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/commercial district|qq=|1=prosperous area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66364404}} with nearby landmarks such as the [[:en:NHK Broadcasting Center|NHK Broadcasting Center]] and Shibuya Ward Office.<ref>[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map]</ref> * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in [[:en:1909|Meiji 42]] during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the [[:en:1920|1920]] flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udagawa River|qq=|1=udagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449570}}.<ref group="note">During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds.</ref>
== Parish District ==
The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * [[:en:Jinnan, Shibuya|Jinnan]] * [[:en:Udagawachō|Udagawachō]] (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya 1-chome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 6-chome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 1-chome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshita Street|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Takeshita Street|WD=Q3179287}} in Harajuku)

[[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo.
== History ==
The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munafuda|qq=}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in [[:en:1660|1660]] and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} (compiled between [[:en:1810|1810]] and [[:en:1830|1830]])<ref>{{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}}</ref> indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era (1469–1486)]]. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.<ref name="A">[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines]</ref>
The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:1997|1997]] with a modern design by the architectural firm led by [[:en:Kiyonori Kikutake|Kikutake Kiyonori]].
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine honors the following main deities:<ref name="A"></ref> * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (associated with [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (another name for [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]) * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omiyanome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}} * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (consort of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a deity of [[:en:Longevity|longevity]] and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arts|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}})
== Features ==
* The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|1=Shibuya Kōen-dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561683}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire-dori Avenue|1=Fire Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280335}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/commercial district|1=prosperous area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66364404}} with nearby landmarks such as the [[:en:NHK Broadcasting Center|NHK Broadcasting Center]] and Shibuya Ward Office.<ref>[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map]</ref> * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in [[:en:1909|Meiji 42]] during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the [[:en:1920|1920]] flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udagawa River|1=udagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449570}}.<ref group="note">During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds.</ref>
== Parish District ==
The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * [[:en:Jinnan, Shibuya|Jinnan]] * [[:en:Udagawachō|Udagawachō]] (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya 1-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 6-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 1-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshita Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Takeshita Street|WD=Q3179287}} in Harajuku)

The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:1997|1997]] with a modern design by the architectural firm led by [[:en:Kiyonori Kikutake|Kikutake Kiyonori]].

The shrine honors the following main deities:<ref name="A"></ref> * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (associated with [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (another name for [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]) * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omiyanome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}} * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (consort of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a deity of [[:en:Longevity|longevity]] and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arts|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}})
== Features ==
* The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|1=Shibuya Kōen-dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561683}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire-dori Avenue|1=Fire Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280335}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/commercial district|1=prosperous area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66364404}} with nearby landmarks such as the [[:en:NHK Broadcasting Center|NHK Broadcasting Center]] and Shibuya Ward Office.<ref>[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map]</ref> * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in [[:en:1909|Meiji 42]] during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the [[:en:1920|1920]] flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udagawa River|1=udagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449570}}.<ref group="note">During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds.</ref>
== Parish District ==
The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * [[:en:Jinnan, Shibuya|Jinnan]] * [[:en:Udagawachō|Udagawachō]] (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya 1-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 6-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 1-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshita Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Takeshita Street|WD=Q3179287}} in Harajuku)
== Nearest Stations ==
; [[:en:Shibuya Station|Shibuya Station]] (major terminal station) : Served by: * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]: [[:en:Yamanote Line|Yamanote Line]], [[:en:Saikyō Line|Saikyō Line]], [[:en:Shōnan–Shinjuku Line|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line]], [[:en:Narita Express|Narita Express]] * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyū Corporation|1=Tokyu Railways|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80229029}}: [[:en:Tōyoko Line|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]], [[:en:Den-en-toshi Line|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line]] * [[:en:Keio Corporation|Keiō Corporation]]: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō Inokashira Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京王井の頭線|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京王井の頭線|1=Keiō Inokashira Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191300}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京地下鉄|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京地下鉄|1=Tokyo Metro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682894}}: [[:en:Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|Ginza Line]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro Hanzōmon Line|Hanzōmon Line]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro Fukutoshin Line|Fukutoshin Line]]

[[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo.
== History ==
The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munafuda|qq=}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in [[:en:1660|1660]] and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} (compiled between [[:en:1810|1810]] and [[:en:1830|1830]])<ref>{{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}}</ref> indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era (1469–1486)]]. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.<ref name="A">[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines]</ref>
The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:1997|1997]] with a modern design by the architectural firm led by [[:en:Kiyonori Kikutake|Kikutake Kiyonori]].
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine honors the following main deities:<ref name="A"></ref> * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (associated with [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (another name for [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]) * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omiyanome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}} * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (consort of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a deity of [[:en:Longevity|longevity]] and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arts|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}})
== Features ==
* The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|1=Shibuya Kōen-dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561683}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire-dori Avenue|1=Fire Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280335}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/commercial district|1=prosperous area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66364404}} with nearby landmarks such as the [[:en:NHK Broadcasting Center|NHK Broadcasting Center]] and Shibuya Ward Office.<ref>[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map]</ref> * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in [[:en:1909|Meiji 42]] during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the [[:en:1920|1920]] flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udagawa River|1=udagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449570}}.<ref group="note">During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds.</ref>
== Parish District ==
The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * [[:en:Jinnan, Shibuya|Jinnan]] * [[:en:Udagawachō|Udagawachō]] (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya 1-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 6-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 1-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshita Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Takeshita Street|WD=Q3179287}} in Harajuku)

[[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]] '''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo.
== History ==
The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munafuda|qq=}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in [[:en:1660|1660]] and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} (compiled between [[:en:1810|1810]] and [[:en:1830|1830]])<ref>{{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}}</ref> indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era (1469–1486)]]. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.<ref name="A">[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines]</ref>
The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:1997|1997]] with a modern design by the architectural firm led by [[:en:Kiyonori Kikutake|Kikutake Kiyonori]].
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine honors the following main deities:<ref name="A"></ref> * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (associated with [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (another name for [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]) * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omiyanome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}} * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (consort of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]) * {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a deity of [[:en:Longevity|longevity]] and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arts|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}})
== Features ==
* The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|1=Shibuya Kōen-dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561683}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire-dori Avenue|1=Fire Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280335}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/commercial district|1=prosperous area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66364404}} with nearby landmarks such as the [[:en:NHK Broadcasting Center|NHK Broadcasting Center]] and Shibuya Ward Office.<ref>[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map]</ref> * Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in [[:en:1909|Meiji 42]] during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the [[:en:1920|1920]] flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udagawa River|1=udagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449570}}.<ref group="note">During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds.</ref>
== Parish District ==
The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya: * [[:en:Jinnan, Shibuya|Jinnan]] * [[:en:Udagawachō|Udagawachō]] (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15) * {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya 1-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 21–23) * {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 6-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27) * {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 1-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshita Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Takeshita Street|WD=Q3179287}} in Harajuku)
== Nearest Stations ==
; [[:en:Shibuya Station|Shibuya Station]] (major terminal station) : Served by: * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]: [[:en:Yamanote Line|Yamanote Line]], [[:en:Saikyō Line|Saikyō Line]], [[:en:Shōnan–Shinjuku Line|Shōnan-Shinjuku Line]], [[:en:Narita Express|Narita Express]] * {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyū Corporation|1=Tokyu Railways|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80229029}}: [[:en:Tōyoko Line|Tōkyū Tōyoko Line]], [[:en:Den-en-toshi Line|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line]] * [[:en:Keio Corporation|Keiō Corporation]]: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō Inokashira Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京王井の頭線|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京王井の頭線|1=Keiō Inokashira Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191300}} * {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京地下鉄|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京地下鉄|1=Tokyo Metro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682894}}: [[:en:Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|Ginza Line]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro Hanzōmon Line|Hanzōmon Line]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro Fukutoshin Line|Fukutoshin Line]]
== References ==
=== Notes === {{Notelist}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[:en:Shibuya (district)|Shibuya]] - [[:en:Jinnan, Shibuya|Jinnan]] - {{ill|Shibuya Ward Office|ja|渋谷区役所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Ward Office|1=Shibuya City Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561690}} * [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto]] - {{ill|Tokyo Shrine Association|ja|東京都神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Shrine Association|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} * {{ill|Deities in Shinto|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deities in Shinto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ|1=Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q346403}} - [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]] * {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|1=Shibuya Kōen-dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561683}} - {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire-dori Avenue|1=Fire Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280335}} * {{ill|Spain-zaka|ja|スペイン坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spain-zaka|1=Spain-zaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5365183}} * {{ill|Cultural Village Street|ja|文化村通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Village Street|1=Bunkamura Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11246527}} * [[:en:Dōgenzaka|Dogenzaka]] * {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|1=Miyamasuzaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455210}}
