[[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]]
'''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo.

== History ==

The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munafuda|qq=}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|en|1660|WD=Q7001|lt=1660}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|en|1810|WD=Q6922|lt=1810}} and {{ill|1830|en|1830|WD=Q7576|lt=1830}})<ref>{{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}}</ref> indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Civilization|en|Civilization|WD=Q8432|lt=Bunmei era (1469–1486)}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.<ref name="A">[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines]</ref>

The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|en|1997|WD=Q2088|lt=1997}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kiyonori Kikutake|en|Kiyonori Kikutake|WD=Q1352623|lt=Kikutake Kiyonori}}.

== Enshrined Deities ==

The shrine honors the following main deities:<ref name="A"></ref>
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Ōkami}})
* {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}})
* {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omiyanome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}
* {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}})
* {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a deity of {{ill|Longevity|en|Longevity|WD=Q1066907|lt=longevity}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arts|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}})

== Features ==

* The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|1=Shibuya Kōen-dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561683}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire-dori Avenue|1=Fire Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280335}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/commercial district|1=prosperous area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66364404}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|en|NHK Broadcasting Center|WD=Q1070546|lt=NHK Broadcasting Center}} and Shibuya Ward Office.<ref>[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map]</ref>
* Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|en|1909|WD=Q2057|lt=Meiji 42}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|en|1920|WD=Q2155|lt=1920}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udagawa River|1=udagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449570}}.<ref group="note">During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds.</ref>

== Parish District ==

The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya:
* {{ill|Jinnan, Shibuya|en|Jinnan, Shibuya|WD=Q11588784|lt=Jinnan}}
* {{ill|Udagawachō|en|Udagawachō|WD=Q10946241|lt=Udagawachō}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15)
* {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya 1-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 21–23)
* {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 6-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27)
* {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 1-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshita Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Takeshita Street|WD=Q3179287}} in Harajuku)

* {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyū Corporation|1=Tokyu Railways|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80229029}}: {{ill|Tōyoko Line|en|Tōyoko Line|WD=Q1192468|lt=Tōkyū Tōyoko Line}}, {{ill|Den-en-toshi Line|en|Den-en-toshi Line|WD=Q1190086|lt=Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line}}
* {{ill|Keio Corporation|en|Keio Corporation|WD=Q1332506|lt=Keiō Corporation}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō Inokashira Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京王井の頭線|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京王井の頭線|1=Keiō Inokashira Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191300}}
* {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京地下鉄|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京地下鉄|1=Tokyo Metro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682894}}: {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|en|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|WD=Q1073046|lt=Ginza Line}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metro Hanzōmon Line|en|Tokyo Metro Hanzōmon Line|WD=Q1376030|lt=Hanzōmon Line}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metro Fukutoshin Line|en|Tokyo Metro Fukutoshin Line|WD=Q1332908|lt=Fukutoshin Line}}

[[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]]
'''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo.

== History ==

The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munafuda|qq=}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|en|1660|WD=Q7001|lt=1660}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|en|1810|WD=Q6922|lt=1810}} and {{ill|1830|en|1830|WD=Q7576|lt=1830}})<ref>{{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}}</ref> indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Civilization|en|Civilization|WD=Q8432|lt=Bunmei era (1469–1486)}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.<ref name="A">[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines]</ref>

The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|en|1997|WD=Q2088|lt=1997}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kiyonori Kikutake|en|Kiyonori Kikutake|WD=Q1352623|lt=Kikutake Kiyonori}}.

== Enshrined Deities ==

The shrine honors the following main deities:<ref name="A"></ref>
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Ōkami}})
* {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}})
* {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omiyanome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}
* {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}})
* {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a deity of {{ill|Longevity|en|Longevity|WD=Q1066907|lt=longevity}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arts|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}})

== Features ==

* The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|1=Shibuya Kōen-dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561683}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire-dori Avenue|1=Fire Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280335}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/commercial district|1=prosperous area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66364404}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|en|NHK Broadcasting Center|WD=Q1070546|lt=NHK Broadcasting Center}} and Shibuya Ward Office.<ref>[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map]</ref>
* Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|en|1909|WD=Q2057|lt=Meiji 42}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|en|1920|WD=Q2155|lt=1920}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udagawa River|1=udagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449570}}.<ref group="note">During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds.</ref>

== Parish District ==

The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya:
* {{ill|Jinnan, Shibuya|en|Jinnan, Shibuya|WD=Q11588784|lt=Jinnan}}
* {{ill|Udagawachō|en|Udagawachō|WD=Q10946241|lt=Udagawachō}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15)
* {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya 1-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 21–23)
* {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 6-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27)
* {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 1-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshita Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Takeshita Street|WD=Q3179287}} in Harajuku)

== Nearest Stations ==

; {{ill|Shibuya Station|en|Shibuya Station|WD=Q428811|lt=Shibuya Station}} (major terminal station)
: Served by:
* {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East}}: {{ill|Yamanote Line|en|Yamanote Line|WD=Q693036|lt=Yamanote Line}}, {{ill|Saikyō Line|en|Saikyō Line|WD=Q1372061|lt=Saikyō Line}}, {{ill|Shōnan–Shinjuku Line|en|Shōnan–Shinjuku Line|WD=Q1323851|lt=Shōnan-Shinjuku Line}}, {{ill|Narita Express|en|Narita Express|WD=Q1369067|lt=Narita Express}}
* {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyū Corporation|1=Tokyu Railways|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80229029}}: {{ill|Tōyoko Line|en|Tōyoko Line|WD=Q1192468|lt=Tōkyū Tōyoko Line}}, {{ill|Den-en-toshi Line|en|Den-en-toshi Line|WD=Q1190086|lt=Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line}}
* {{ill|Keio Corporation|en|Keio Corporation|WD=Q1332506|lt=Keiō Corporation}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō Inokashira Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京王井の頭線|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京王井の頭線|1=Keiō Inokashira Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191300}}
* {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京地下鉄|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京地下鉄|1=Tokyo Metro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682894}}: {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|en|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|WD=Q1073046|lt=Ginza Line}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metro Hanzōmon Line|en|Tokyo Metro Hanzōmon Line|WD=Q1376030|lt=Hanzōmon Line}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metro Fukutoshin Line|en|Tokyo Metro Fukutoshin Line|WD=Q1332908|lt=Fukutoshin Line}}

[[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]]
'''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo.

== History ==

The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munafuda|qq=}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|en|1660|WD=Q7001|lt=1660|1=1660}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|en|1810|WD=Q6922|lt=1810|1=1810}} and {{ill|1830|en|1830|WD=Q7576|lt=1830|1=1830}})<ref>{{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}}</ref> indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Civilization|en|Civilization|WD=Q8432|lt=Bunmei era (1469–1486)|1=Civilization}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.<ref name="A">[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines]</ref>

The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|en|1997|WD=Q2088|lt=1997|1=1997}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kiyonori Kikutake|en|Kiyonori Kikutake|WD=Q1352623|lt=Kikutake Kiyonori|1=Kiyonori Kikutake}}.

== Enshrined Deities ==

The shrine honors the following main deities:<ref name="A"></ref>
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Ōkami|1=Inari Ōkami}})
* {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}})
* {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omiyanome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}
* {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}})
* {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a deity of {{ill|Longevity|en|Longevity|WD=Q1066907|lt=longevity|1=Longevity}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arts|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}})

== Features ==

* The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|1=Shibuya Kōen-dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561683}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire-dori Avenue|1=Fire Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280335}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/commercial district|1=prosperous area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66364404}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|en|NHK Broadcasting Center|WD=Q1070546|lt=NHK Broadcasting Center|1=NHK Broadcasting Center}} and Shibuya Ward Office.<ref>[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map]</ref>
* Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|en|1909|WD=Q2057|lt=Meiji 42|1=1909}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|en|1920|WD=Q2155|lt=1920|1=1920}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udagawa River|1=udagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449570}}.<ref group="note">During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds.</ref>

== Parish District ==

The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya:
* {{ill|Jinnan, Shibuya|en|Jinnan, Shibuya|WD=Q11588784|lt=Jinnan|1=Jinnan, Shibuya}}
* {{ill|Udagawachō|en|Udagawachō|WD=Q10946241|lt=Udagawachō|1=Udagawachō}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15)

[[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]]
'''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo.

== History ==

The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munafuda|qq=}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|en|1660|WD=Q7001|lt=1660|1=1660}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|en|1810|WD=Q6922|lt=1810|1=1810}} and {{ill|1830|en|1830|WD=Q7576|lt=1830|1=1830}})<ref>{{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}}</ref> indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Civilization|en|Civilization|WD=Q8432|lt=Bunmei era (1469–1486)|1=Civilization}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.<ref name="A">[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines]</ref>

The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|en|1997|WD=Q2088|lt=1997|1=1997}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kiyonori Kikutake|en|Kiyonori Kikutake|WD=Q1352623|lt=Kikutake Kiyonori|1=Kiyonori Kikutake}}.

== Enshrined Deities ==

The shrine honors the following main deities:<ref name="A"></ref>
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Ōkami|1=Inari Ōkami}})
* {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}})
* {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omiyanome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}
* {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}})
* {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a deity of {{ill|Longevity|en|Longevity|WD=Q1066907|lt=longevity|1=Longevity}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arts|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}})

== Features ==

* The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|1=Shibuya Kōen-dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561683}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire-dori Avenue|1=Fire Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280335}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/commercial district|1=prosperous area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66364404}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|en|NHK Broadcasting Center|WD=Q1070546|lt=NHK Broadcasting Center|1=NHK Broadcasting Center}} and Shibuya Ward Office.<ref>[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map]</ref>
* Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|en|1909|WD=Q2057|lt=Meiji 42|1=1909}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|en|1920|WD=Q2155|lt=1920|1=1920}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udagawa River|1=udagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449570}}.<ref group="note">During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds.</ref>

== Parish District ==

The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya:
* {{ill|Jinnan, Shibuya|en|Jinnan, Shibuya|WD=Q11588784|lt=Jinnan|1=Jinnan, Shibuya}}
* {{ill|Udagawachō|en|Udagawachō|WD=Q10946241|lt=Udagawachō|1=Udagawachō}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15)
* {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya 1-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 21–23)
* {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 6-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27)
* {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 1-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshita Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Takeshita Street|WD=Q3179287}} in Harajuku)

[[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]]
'''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo.

== History ==

The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munafuda|qq=}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|en|1660|WD=Q7001|lt=1660|1=1660}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|en|1810|WD=Q6922|lt=1810|1=1810}} and {{ill|1830|en|1830|WD=Q7576|lt=1830|1=1830}})<ref>{{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}}</ref> indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Civilization|en|Civilization|WD=Q8432|lt=Bunmei era (1469–1486)|1=Civilization}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.<ref name="A">[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines]</ref>

The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|en|1997|WD=Q2088|lt=1997|1=1997}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kiyonori Kikutake|en|Kiyonori Kikutake|WD=Q1352623|lt=Kikutake Kiyonori|1=Kiyonori Kikutake}}.

== Enshrined Deities ==

The shrine honors the following main deities:<ref name="A"></ref>
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Ōkami|1=Inari Ōkami}})
* {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}})
* {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omiyanome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}
* {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}})
* {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a deity of {{ill|Longevity|en|Longevity|WD=Q1066907|lt=longevity|1=Longevity}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arts|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}})

== Features ==

* The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|1=Shibuya Kōen-dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561683}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire-dori Avenue|1=Fire Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280335}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/commercial district|1=prosperous area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66364404}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|en|NHK Broadcasting Center|WD=Q1070546|lt=NHK Broadcasting Center|1=NHK Broadcasting Center}} and Shibuya Ward Office.<ref>[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map]</ref>
* Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|en|1909|WD=Q2057|lt=Meiji 42|1=1909}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|en|1920|WD=Q2155|lt=1920|1=1920}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udagawa River|1=udagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449570}}.<ref group="note">During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds.</ref>

== Parish District ==

The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya:
* {{ill|Jinnan, Shibuya|en|Jinnan, Shibuya|WD=Q11588784|lt=Jinnan|1=Jinnan, Shibuya}}
* {{ill|Udagawachō|en|Udagawachō|WD=Q10946241|lt=Udagawachō|1=Udagawachō}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15)
* {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya 1-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 21–23)
* {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 6-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27)
* {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 1-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshita Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Takeshita Street|WD=Q3179287}} in Harajuku)

== Nearest Stations ==

* {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyū Corporation|1=Tokyu Railways|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80229029}}: {{ill|Tōyoko Line|en|Tōyoko Line|WD=Q1192468|lt=Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|1=Tōyoko Line}}, {{ill|Den-en-toshi Line|en|Den-en-toshi Line|WD=Q1190086|lt=Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|1=Den-en-toshi Line}}
* {{ill|Keio Corporation|en|Keio Corporation|WD=Q1332506|lt=Keiō Corporation|1=Keio Corporation}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō Inokashira Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京王井の頭線|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京王井の頭線|1=Keio Inokashira Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191300}}

[[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]]
'''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo.

== History ==

The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munafuda|qq=}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|en|1660|WD=Q7001|lt=1660|1=1660}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|en|1810|WD=Q6922|lt=1810|1=1810}} and {{ill|1830|en|1830|WD=Q7576|lt=1830|1=1830}})<ref>{{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}}</ref> indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Civilization|en|Civilization|WD=Q8432|lt=Bunmei era (1469–1486)|1=Civilization}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.<ref name="A">[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines]</ref>

The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|en|1997|WD=Q2088|lt=1997|1=1997}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kiyonori Kikutake|en|Kiyonori Kikutake|WD=Q1352623|lt=Kikutake Kiyonori|1=Kiyonori Kikutake}}.

== Enshrined Deities ==

The shrine honors the following main deities:<ref name="A"></ref>
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Ōkami|1=Inari Ōkami}})
* {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}})
* {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omiyanome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}
* {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}})
* {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a deity of {{ill|Longevity|en|Longevity|WD=Q1066907|lt=longevity|1=Longevity}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arts|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}})

== Features ==

* The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|1=Shibuya Kōen-dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561683}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire-dori Avenue|1=Fire Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280335}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/commercial district|1=prosperous area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66364404}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|en|NHK Broadcasting Center|WD=Q1070546|lt=NHK Broadcasting Center|1=NHK Broadcasting Center}} and Shibuya Ward Office.<ref>[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map]</ref>
* Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|en|1909|WD=Q2057|lt=Meiji 42|1=1909}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|en|1920|WD=Q2155|lt=1920|1=1920}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udagawa River|1=udagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449570}}.<ref group="note">During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds.</ref>

== Parish District ==

The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya:
* {{ill|Jinnan, Shibuya|en|Jinnan, Shibuya|WD=Q11588784|lt=Jinnan|1=Jinnan, Shibuya}}
* {{ill|Udagawachō|en|Udagawachō|WD=Q10946241|lt=Udagawachō|1=Udagawachō}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15)
* {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya 1-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 21–23)
* {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 6-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27)
* {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 1-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshita Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Takeshita Street|WD=Q3179287}} in Harajuku)

[[File:Kitaya-Inari-Shrine-Shibuya-03.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii Gate]]
'''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo.

== History ==

The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munafuda|qq=}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|en|1660|qid=Q7001|lt=1660|1=1660}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11503943}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|en|1810|qid=Q6922|lt=1810|1=1810}} and {{ill|1830|en|1830|qid=Q7576|lt=1830|1=1830}})<ref>{{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}}</ref> indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Civilization|en|Civilization|qid=Q8432|lt=Bunmei era (1469–1486)|1=Civilization}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.<ref name="A">[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines]</ref>

The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|en|1997|qid=Q2088|lt=1997|1=1997}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kiyonori Kikutake|en|Kiyonori Kikutake|qid=Q1352623|lt=Kikutake Kiyonori|1=Kiyonori Kikutake}}.

== Enshrined Deities ==

The shrine honors the following main deities:<ref name="A"></ref>
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之霊大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (associated with {{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|qid=Q719665|lt=Inari Ōkami|1=Inari Ōkami}})
* {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大己貴大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}})
* {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Omiyanome|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja|大宮比賣大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omiyanome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}
* {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}})
* {{ill|Otanomikoto|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a deity of {{ill|Longevity|en|Longevity|qid=Q1066907|lt=longevity|1=Longevity}} and {{ill|arts|ja|芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arts|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11615913}})

== Features ==

* The shrine is located between {{ill|Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|ja|渋谷公園通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Koen-dori Avenue|1=Shibuya Kōen-dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11561683}} to the west and {{ill|Fire-dori Avenue|ja|ファイヤー通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire-dori Avenue|1=Fire Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11280335}} to the east. The surrounding area is a bustling {{ill|commercial district|ja|繁華街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/commercial district|1=prosperous area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q66364404}} with nearby landmarks such as the {{ill|NHK Broadcasting Center|en|NHK Broadcasting Center|qid=Q1070546|lt=NHK Broadcasting Center|1=NHK Broadcasting Center}} and Shibuya Ward Office.<ref>[http://www.koen-dori.com/townmap/a3.html 渋谷公園通り Town Map]</ref>
* Notable monuments in the precinct include a stone erected by farmers in {{ill|1909|en|1909|qid=Q2057|lt=Meiji 42|1=1909}} during the establishment of a training ground and a flood control monument commemorating the {{ill|1920|en|1920|qid=Q2155|lt=1920|1=1920}} flood of the {{ill|Udagawa River|ja|宇田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udagawa River|1=udagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11449570}}.<ref group="note">During the Meiji period, the surrounding area was under the management of the Ministry of War and used for training grounds.</ref>

== Parish District ==

The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya:
* {{ill|Jinnan, Shibuya|en|Jinnan, Shibuya|qid=Q11588784|lt=Jinnan|1=Jinnan, Shibuya}}
* {{ill|Udagawachō|en|Udagawachō|qid=Q10946241|lt=Udagawachō|1=Udagawachō}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15)
* {{ill|Shibuya 1-chome|ja|渋谷一丁目|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya 1-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 21–23)
* {{ill|Jingūmae 6-chome|ja|神宮前六丁目|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 6-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 12, 14, parts of 5–19, 21–26, and parts of 27)
* {{ill|Jingūmae 1-chome|ja|神宮前一丁目|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūmae 1-chome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Blocks 7–9, parts of 14, and 16–20, including {{ill|Takeshita Street|ja|竹下通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshita Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Takeshita Street|qid=Q3179287}} in Harajuku)

== Nearest Stations ==

; {{ill|Shibuya Station|en|Shibuya Station|qid=Q428811|lt=Shibuya Station|1=Shibuya Station}} (major terminal station)

* {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}}: {{ill|Yamanote Line|en|Yamanote Line|qid=Q693036|lt=Yamanote Line|1=Yamanote Line}}, {{ill|Saikyō Line|en|Saikyō Line|qid=Q1372061|lt=Saikyō Line|1=Saikyō Line}}, {{ill|Shōnan–Shinjuku Line|en|Shōnan–Shinjuku Line|qid=Q1323851|lt=Shōnan-Shinjuku Line|1=Shōnan–Shinjuku Line}}, {{ill|Narita Express|en|Narita Express|qid=Q1369067|lt=Narita Express|1=Narita Express}}
* {{ill|Tōkyū Corporation|ja|東急電鉄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyū Corporation|1=Tokyu Railways|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q80229029}}: {{ill|Tōyoko Line|en|Tōyoko Line|qid=Q1192468|lt=Tōkyū Tōyoko Line|1=Tōyoko Line}}, {{ill|Den-en-toshi Line|en|Den-en-toshi Line|qid=Q1190086|lt=Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|1=Den-en-toshi Line}}
* {{ill|Keio Corporation|en|Keio Corporation|qid=Q1332506|lt=Keiō Corporation|1=Keio Corporation}}: {{ill|Keiō Inokashira Line|ja|井の頭線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō Inokashira Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京王井の頭線|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京王井の頭線|1=Keio Inokashira Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1191300}}
* {{ill|Tokyo Metro|ja|東京メトロ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京地下鉄|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京地下鉄|1=Tokyo Metro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q682894}}: {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|en|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|qid=Q1073046|lt=Ginza Line|1=Ginza Line}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metro Hanzōmon Line|en|Tokyo Metro Hanzōmon Line|qid=Q1376030|lt=Hanzōmon Line|1=Hanzōmon Line}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metro Fukutoshin Line|en|Tokyo Metro Fukutoshin Line|qid=Q1332908|lt=Fukutoshin Line|1=Fukutoshin Line}}

'''Kitaya Inari Shrine''' (北谷稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Jinnan, Shibuya, Tokyo.

The exact year of establishment is unknown. However, records such as the {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munafuda|qq=}} from the reconstruction after significant damage in {{ill|1660|en|1660|qid=Q7001|lt=1660|1=1660}} and descriptions in the {{ill|Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Edited Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11503943}} (compiled between {{ill|1810|en|1810|qid=Q6922|lt=1810|1=1810}} and {{ill|1830|en|1830|qid=Q7576|lt=1830|1=1830}})<ref>{{cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 10, Toshima District, Upper Shibuya Village|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763976/81}}}}</ref> indicate that the shrine may have been established as early as the {{ill|Civilization|en|Civilization|qid=Q8432|lt=Bunmei era (1469–1486)|1=Civilization}}. The shrine was possibly moved from Suruga Province and enshrined in the northeastern corner of the premises at that time.<ref name="A">[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/06_shibuya/list.html Tokyo Shrine Association: Shibuya Ward Shrines]</ref>

The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1997|en|1997|qid=Q2088|lt=1997|1=1997}} with a modern design by the architectural firm led by {{ill|Kiyonori Kikutake|en|Kiyonori Kikutake|qid=Q1352623|lt=Kikutake Kiyonori|1=Kiyonori Kikutake}}.

== Parish District ==

The shrine serves the following areas in Shibuya:
* {{ill|Jinnan, Shibuya|en|Jinnan, Shibuya|qid=Q11588784|lt=Jinnan|1=Jinnan, Shibuya}}
* {{ill|Udagawachō|en|Udagawachō|qid=Q10946241|lt=Udagawachō|1=Udagawachō}} (Blocks 1–13, parts of 14 and 15)
